races.race_name: Bear's Big Birthday EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Thunda Struck
runners.spotlight: Promising second at Ripon on debut (6f, good) but she failed to build on it at Leicester (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago; others stronger.
runners.comment: Failed to build on debut promise last time; others open to greater progress

runners.name: Regal Chita
runners.spotlight: The penny dropped late on at Newmarket on his debut 16 days ago (6f, good), passing a few in the final furlong to snatch third close home; should be more streetwise today; key player.
runners.comment: Good late headway to take third on recent Newmarket debut (6f, good); plenty more to come

runners.name: Poppy Foxy
runners.spotlight: 21,000gns yearling; by Bungle Inthejungle; second foal of a 1m/1m2f winner (RPR 82), herself a half-sister to a Grade 3 winner in the US; stable yet to strike with 2yos this year (as of Friday evening) but she's bred for the job.
runners.comment: 21,000gns yearling; dam a 1m/1m2f winner; worth a market check

runners.name: Big Bad Storm
runners.spotlight: 3,000gns foal, £27,000 yearling, £15,000 breeze-up 2yo; by Ubettabelieveit; fifth foal; half-brother to 6f-8.6f 2yo winner Isobar Wind (including AW; RPR 69); dam 5.7f/6f winner (including 2yo; 78), half-sister to useful 6f/7f winner Shimmering Dawn; stable had a newcomer win at the Craven meeting; not discounted.
runners.comment: £15,000 breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to a 2yo winner; stable off the mark with their 2yos

runners.name: Grayrobin
runners.spotlight: 43,000euros yearling, resold £36,000 yearling; by Havana Grey; first foal of a 5f AW winner, herself a sister to a 5f winner from a good sprinting family; stable's 2yos have made bright start to the year; betting to guide.
runners.comment: £36,000 yearling; first foal of a 5f winner from a good sprinting family; betting to guide

runners.name: Treasurer
runners.spotlight: 40,000euros yearling; twelfth foal; half-brother to seven winners including Gordon Bennett (5f/6f; RPR 102), Lorelei Rock (5f 2yo including Listed; 94) and Es Raco (Swedish Listed-placed 5f/6f including 2yo; 92); dam French 7.5f AW winner, half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Regime; stable have had some 2yo success this year; market useful.
runners.comment: 40,000euros half-brother to seven winners; yard have had 2yo success in 2026; check market

runners.name: Bated Benevolence
runners.spotlight: Second foal; Bated Breath half-sister to 7f AW winner Galileo Charm (RPR 68); dam unraced sister to useful 7f/7.7f winner One Nation; stable off the mark with 2yos (second time out); check the betting.
runners.comment: Half-sister to minor 7f AW winner Galileo Charm; betting should be revealing

runners.name: Whiskey Bent
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros yearling, 32,500gns breeze-up 2yo; by Hello Youmzain; first foal of a French maiden from a useful family; stable off the mark with 2yos for the year; market to guide.
runners.comment: 32,500gns breeze-up 2yo; dam a maiden from a useful family; betting to guide

runners.name: Harley
runners.spotlight: Never a threat to the principals but he wasn't beaten far when fifth of six on his Hamilton debut (6f, good) 16 days ago; could leave that well behind in time.
runners.comment: Never threatened on recent Hamilton debut but there was some promise; can do better

runners.name: Zohar
runners.spotlight: 25-1 when ninth of 13 at Newmarket 16 days ago (6f, good), finishing 4l behind Regal Chita; work to do to turn the tables; stable also run Treasurer.
runners.comment: 4l behind Regal Chita when 9th on Newmarket debut; improvement essential; trainer runs two

runners.name: Carry On Chaos
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and never in the hunt on his recent Redcar debut (6f, good); may need more time.
runners.comment: 40-1 and never in the hunt on his recent Redcar debut (6f, good); may need more time

races.race_name: Construction Day 14th Oct Handicap

runners.name: Legacy Rock
runners.spotlight: 2-2 since upped to 1m, winning a pair of Class 5 events at Windsor, the latest eight days ago in comfortable fashion; 5lb higher in his hat-trick bid but he hasn't reached his ceiling just yet.
runners.comment: 2-2 over 1m and still open to further progress; good chance to complete the hat-trick

runners.name: Daretobedifferent
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden but she has been competitive in her last two handicaps and should remain so; could have more to offer at the trip; each-way shout once again.
runners.comment: 0-11 but good runs on her last two starts and unexposed as a miler; each-way shout again

runners.name: Spacewoman
runners.spotlight: Promise in novice/maiden company but she is yet to kick on in handicaps, including when only eighth over C&D on her reappearance five weeks ago; another 3lb lower today and cheekpieces are given a go; others look safer.
runners.comment: Yet to match maiden/novice form in handicaps; now tried in cheekpieces

runners.name: High Chieftain
runners.spotlight: No immediate improvement for the switch to handicaps and a longer trip (8.6f) when fourth of five at Wolverhampton in February; still has time to do better and cheekpieces could give him a lift.
runners.comment: Fourth of five on handicap debut in Februray (8.6f, AW); cheekpieces now added; unexposed

runners.name: Blue Celestial
runners.spotlight: In the frame in four of her six runs since joining James Tate, keeping on well for second at Kempton (1m) 11 days ago; more to prove on turf though and others look stronger.
runners.comment: Chance on her AW best but she has more to prove on the grass

runners.name: Space Moon
runners.spotlight: She has run creditably in her three handicap starts without suggesting she was ahead of her mark; others bring stronger credentials.
runners.comment: She has run okay in handicaps without suggesting she was ahead of her mark

runners.name: Sweet Mabel
runners.spotlight: Modest form in four runs for David Loughnane, including when blowing the start on her handicap debut (1m, AW) in January; something to prove on her turf and stable debut (same owners).
runners.comment: Modest form for David Loughnane; improvement required on stable/turf debut
runners.owner: E Murchan, K McConnell, A & P Shaw

races.race_name: When You Wish Upon A Star Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lucky Camino
runners.spotlight: Looked a bit unfortunate not to make a winning start to his career over C&D (good to soft) 12 days ago, rallying in the final furlong having been tight for room against the fair rail when launching his challenge; stable going well; solid chance.
runners.comment: Unlucky in running when second over C&D 12 days ago (good to soft); obvious chance

runners.name: Dark Whisper
runners.spotlight: Promising start to his career when second of ten, beaten 5.5l, in the Wood Ditton at Newmarket last month (1m, good; newcomers race); the winner of that race is a smart prospect but the results of those in behind have been mixed; open to plenty of improvement and he looks a strong contender.
runners.comment: 5.5l second behind a smart prospect in the Wood Ditton; more to come; strong contender

runners.name: Leonardo Blu
runners.spotlight: Well-bred 3yo who was  a promising second of six, despite looking badly in need of the experience in the closing stages, in a 1m novice Doncaster four weeks ago (good to firm); that form has been boosted since by the winner and the fourth; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Debut 2nd at Doncaster, when hanging badly late on, has been boosted; plenty more to come
runners.owner: J Collins, P Moorhead, C & J Cooley

runners.name: Livemore
runners.spotlight: 15,000gns foal, 45,000gns yearling; eighth foal; brother to 7f/1m winner Organ (RPR 92), closely related to three winners including Sound Of Cannons (1m-1m4f including 2yo/Australia; 102) and Ghadbbaan (1m4f-2m; 75); dam Listed-placed 1m1f/1m2f winner (101); has a useful standard to aim at on debut; betting to guide.
runners.comment: Brother to a useful 7f/1m winner & closely related to 3 more winners; market check advised

runners.name: Turton
runners.spotlight: 14-1, slowly away and never in contention in a Southwell novice last month (7f); gelded since; improvement essential to trouble the principals.
runners.comment: 14-1 and never dangerous after a slow start on last month's debut (7f, AW); gelded since

runners.name: Symphony's Song
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two AW runs 425 days apart (the latest her stable debut); minor handicaps on the agenda soon.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two AW runs 425 days apart; one for minor handicaps soon

runners.name: Sunnie Jim
runners.spotlight: 50-1, slowly away and always behind on his Southwell debut ten days ago (1m).
runners.comment: 50-1, slowly away and always behind on his Southwell debut ten days ago (1m)

races.race_name: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Time To Take Off
runners.spotlight: Looked good when winning a C&D maiden on her debut last October (good to soft; form franked); beaten twice in novice company this spring but she moves into handicaps with the potential for more.
runners.comment: Looked good over C&D on 2yo debut; runner-up twice this spring; can do better handicapping

runners.name: Ruby's Profit
runners.spotlight: In good form for most of 2025, winning two 5f handicaps last spring; not quite hit those heights this time around, including from a good draw at Chester 24 days ago (met some trouble in the home straight); today's drop into a Class 4 will help and she is back against her own sex; should go well.
runners.comment: Not found her very best this year but down in class and tackling her own sex today

runners.name: Emerald Harmony
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish mare; ran a cracker on her stable debut at Newcastle in March (5f), beaten a nose by a well-handicapped opponent; mixed fortunes in three runs since; others may have her measure today.
runners.comment: Good start for new yard when 2nd at Newcastle in March; up and down in three runs since

runners.name: Novelette
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of her first four 2yo runs, following a Yarmouth novice win with a cosy success at Sandown last July; flopped on soft ground wn last seen but that isn't the run to judge her on; absent for 275 days but likely there is more in the tank.
runners.comment: Progressive 2yo before flopping on soft ground on final start; can improve again this year
runners.owner: M Tabor, D Smith & Mrs J Magnier

runners.name: Sugar Hill Babe
runners.spotlight: Her front-running C&D win last May came off just 1lb lower; ended 2025 quietly but she returned to form after six months off, keeping on well for second at Musselburgh (5f, good) 32 days ago; takes on some less-exposed 3yos but likely to go well.
runners.comment: C&D winner; promising second at Musselburgh after a six-month break; should go well

runners.name: Wateen
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained by Alice Haynes; highly tried as a 2yo after winning a 5f Lingfield maiden (good ground), running her best race when third of seven in a Listed race at Deauville last August (5f, good to soft); not obviously well treated for her handicap debut but she has joined a stable known for their sprinters and looks worth a market check.
runners.comment: Listed third at 2; not obviously well treated but could improve for the move to R Cowell

runners.name: Chicory
runners.spotlight: All races at 6f, winning a Newbury fillies' maiden last August before being highly tried on her next two starts; disappointing on her seasonal/handicap debut at Southwell last month; drops to 5f on a recovery mission.
runners.comment: Low-key seasonal/handicap debut last month; drops in trip with a point to prove

races.race_name: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Handicap

runners.name: Tazaman
runners.spotlight: A dual winner (1m4f/2m1f) in 2025; off seven months before beating one in 1m4f handicap at Doncaster 29 days ago but should strip sharper here.
runners.comment: Off seven months before beating one at Doncaster 29 days ago but should strip sharper here

runners.name: Tupero
runners.spotlight: French recruit who is in excellent form for his current yard, gaining a second win of 2026 in 1m6f handicap at Redcar 24 days ago; up 4lb but had something in the tank there so he's a player.
runners.comment: Second win of 2026 at Redcar latest; up 4lb but had something in the tank there; player

runners.name: Two Plus Two
runners.spotlight: Back in excellent form after 12 months off and landed 1m6f handicap at Bath nine days ago; up 3lb but this gutsy front-runner is not taken lightly.
runners.comment: Landed Bath h'cap nine days ago; up 3lb but this gutsy front-runner is not taken lightly

runners.name: In The Post
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who arrives in good form, third of seven in 1m4f handicap at Doncaster 15 days ago; not out of things in his bid for a maiden victory.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 4yo who arrives in good form; not out of things in his bid for a maiden win

runners.name: Bulldog Spirit
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D last May for Brian Ellison; yet to fire for his new yard in handicaps at Wolverhampton (1m4f) and Lingfield (2m) this spring though.
runners.comment: Yet to fire in handicaps for his new yard at Wolverhampton and Lingfield (2m) this spring

runners.name: Diamond Bay
runners.spotlight: On a losing run and he was tried in blinkers when a below-par seventh of ten in 1m6f handicap at Goodwood 30 days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: On a losing run and below-par seventh at Goodwood 30 days ago; others appeal more

runners.name: Little Peter
runners.spotlight: A fair 2m winner at his best but he came in last in 2m Newcastle handicap in December; won a point last month though so is no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Last at Newcastle in December; won a point last month though so no forlorn hope

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Sharp Romance
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced filly who won a 1m AW maiden before just missing out on her handicap debut at Newmarket (1m2f, good) where she did well given she saw plenty of daylight ahead of her; only 2lb higher.
runners.comment: Won a 1m AW maiden before just missing out on her handicap debut at Newmarket (1m2f)

runners.name: Galaxy Star
runners.spotlight: Did well in bumpers; won a 1m3f AW maiden and then ran surprisingly well in a 1m4f Listed race; that did her mark no favours but Harry Vigors takes off 7lb and she might do better again.
runners.comment: Fourth in a Listed race; that did her mark no favours but Harry Vigors takes off 7lb

runners.name: Lady Dora Mae
runners.spotlight: Progressing and likeably consistent; dug deep to finish the job off at Windsor (1m, good to firm) last week and gives the impression that she'll be fine over this far (stays 1m1f); respected off 5lb higher.
runners.comment: Progressing and likeably consistent; dug deep to finish the job off at Windsor (1m)

runners.name: Dunkeld Dreamer
runners.spotlight: In form and although beaten 8l into second at Hamilton (1m1f, good) the winner looked way ahead of her mark; has finished runner-up over C&D and can run well again.
runners.comment: Beaten 8l into second at Hamilton (1m1f, good); the winner looked way ahead of her mark

runners.name: Bearin Up
runners.spotlight: Her second win in ten starts when a cosy winner at Newmarket (1m, good); 5lb higher and that was a Class 5 event, but this longer trip might eke out further improvement.
runners.comment: Cosy winner of a Newmarket Class 5; raised 5lb but this longer trip might bring her on

runners.name: Shady Bay
runners.spotlight: Now 311 over C&D after beating four rivals with ease a month ago (good); this 5lb higher mark has proved troublesome in the past but those runs were elsewhere.
runners.comment: Now 311 over C&D after beating four rivals with ease a month ago
runners.owner: P Afia, E Hobson, A Pegley, C&r Russell

runners.name: Silken Bay
runners.spotlight: Progressing when winning a 1m AW maiden (in a first-time hood that's been retained) and third in a Haydock novice; her sixth of seven in a Wolverhampton handicap (8.6f) was a backward step, though.
runners.comment: AW winner who was progressing until finishing only sixth on handicap debut

races.race_name: £9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Leadenhall
runners.spotlight: 6lb lower than when winning at Haydock last summer (1m, good to firm); two creditable runs in defeat this season and he now drops in class.
runners.comment: On a good mark; two encouraging runs this year and he now drops in class

runners.name: Great Blasket
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; 5lb lower than for his last success; ran a solid race on this month's return to turf at Windsor; one to consider.
runners.comment: C&D winner who is on a good mark; sharper for latest run; one to consider

runners.name: The Sweet Escape
runners.spotlight: Saw off 14 rivals when narrowly winning over C&D last month (Class 6); also went close in a Class 6 at Leicester this month but either side of that he was unplaced in Class 5 events; others preferred for the win.
runners.comment: C&D win last month; went close at Leicester on penultimate start; needs more in this grade

runners.name: Prodigal Son
runners.spotlight: Blinkers have helped him this spring, seeing off five rivals at Bath (1m, firm) last month and then finishing close up in third back at Bath (1m2f, firm) 19 days ago; returning to a mile can help and Mason Paetel's claim is a useful one.
runners.comment: 2 good runs in blinkers this year (1m/1m2f, both firm ground); return to 1m a plus; chance

runners.name: Bass Player
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with two good runs but he has been well short of that level in two runs this year, a wind op before the latest making no difference; tongue tie returns; risky despite his reduced mark.
runners.comment: Two disappointing runs this year either side of a wind op; tongue tie returns; risky

runners.name: Penelope Valentine
runners.spotlight: Won two AW handicaps (7f/8.6f) over the winter; the handicapper may have caught up now and she still has to prove she can be as effective on turf.
runners.comment: Better on AW to this point; returns to turf off a tough-enough mark

runners.name: Stardancer
runners.spotlight: Promise in AW novices and again when third of ten at Doncaster on her handicap debut last June (7f, good); disappointed at Yarmouth the following month and absent since; has undergone wind surgery during his layoff; drops in class, up in trip and tongue tied for his return; still has potential.
runners.comment: 0-5 but clear promise over shorter trips; had wind op; new trip/tongue tie; respected

races.race_name: Enter ITV7 For Free Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Leave The Bag In
runners.spotlight: 16-1 and green, made an encouraging start when eighth of 15 in novice at York (6f, good) 17 days ago, going on well at the finish; this Cotai Glory colt is capable of better, especially over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Encouraging start with running-on eighth at York; capable of better, especially over 7f

runners.name: High Hazard
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros foal, £95,000 yearling; Palace Pier colt; dam is an unraced sister to 6f winner Partitia; yard's juveniles are going well so this newcomer must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Palace Pier colt; yard's juveniles going well so this newcomer must enter calculations

runners.name: Sir Sirius
runners.spotlight: 11-1 and very green when a never-dangerous ninth of 15 on his debut in novice at York (6f, good) 17 days ago; this son of Starman fetched 350,000gns as a yearling and it would come as no surprise to see him take a sizeable step forward.
runners.comment: Very green when a debut ninth at York; no surprise to see him take a sizeable step forward
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Alfred Wallace
runners.spotlight: 14-1, shaped well when a debut fifth of ten in novice at Ascot (6f, good to firm) 22 days ago, running on late; this son of Dubawi seems sure to take a step forward over this longer trip; big shout.
runners.comment: Shaped well when debut fifth at Ascot; can step forward over this longer trip; big shout

runners.name: Pikachu
runners.spotlight: 450,000gns yearling; closely related to 1m winner Foniska (Listed; RPR 107) and a half-brother to winners Botagoz (7f/1m; RPR 97) and Boca Queen (Canadian 7f); dam 1m-1m2f winner (Group 2; RPR 113); lots to like on paper so this son of Frankel rates an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Lots to like on paper so this son of Frankel rates an interesting newcomer

runners.name: Fuel The Jet
runners.spotlight: 75,000euros foal, 190,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners Tales Of Wisdom (7f/1m; RPR 103) and Bold Style (Listed-placed 1m; RPR 103); dam unraced half-sister to Group 1 winners Tornado Alert (German 1m2f) and Just Fine (Australian 1m4f); this son of Space Blues needs considering.
runners.comment: Fetched 190,000euros as a yearling and this son of Space Blues needs considering

runners.name: Arc Vince
runners.spotlight: 23,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Don Chicco (Italian 6f-1m Group 2; RPR 110) and Hidden Happiness (Swedish 6f 2yo); dam, unraced half-sister to smart 1m-1m2f winner Khairaat, is out of a half-sister to 1m4f Group 2 winner Maraahel; the market can guide for this son of Ubettabelieveit.
runners.comment: The market can guide for this son of Ubettabelieveit on his debut

runners.name: Rosberg
runners.spotlight: 45,000gns foal, 125,000gns yearling; half-brother to Qatari 12.5f winner Beat The Clock; dam, 1m winner (RPR 74), is a half-sister to 1m/10.6f winner Halcyon Spirit; this Palace Pier colt is in good hands and very much one to consider.
runners.comment: This Palace Pier colt is in good hands and very much one to consider on his first run

runners.name: Whispering Moon
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns foal, 100,000gns yearling; Sea The Moon colt; dam, German 1m1f winner, is a half-sister to German Oaks winner Well Timed; appeals on pedigree so he is another newcomer to consider.
runners.comment: Appeals on pedigree so this Sea The Moon colt is another newcomer to consider
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs J D Cotton

runners.name: Dave's Matey
runners.spotlight: 25-1 and very green when 13th of 15 in maiden at Redcar (6f, good) on his debut 13 days ago; this son of Invincible Army can take a step forward.
runners.comment: Very green when a debut 13th in maiden at Redcar; he can take a step forward

races.race_name: Flutter Proud To Support Young Lives Vs Cancer Handicap

runners.name: Kinnalargy
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 5f juvenile winner; gelded/off eight months and shaped as if the run was needed when 13th in 5f York handicap 16 days ago; no surprise to see him play a prominent role here.
runners.comment: Shaped as if run was needed with York 13th on return; no surprise to see him step forward

runners.name: Noble Vow
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never colt who arrives on the up, getting off the mark in 5.5f Bath maiden before coming home really strongly to follow up in 5f Haydock handicap eight days ago; up 7lb but not taken lightly in his hat-trick bid.
runners.comment: Won at Bath and followed up at Haydock eight days ago; not taken lightly in hat-trick bid

runners.name: Big Fun
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive at two (bagged 5f Musselburgh maiden) and he resumed from nine months off with a good fourth of 14 over C&D last month; possibilities.
runners.comment: Resumed from nine months off with a good fourth of 14 over C&D last month; possibilities

runners.name: Explosive Finnish
runners.spotlight: Son of Pinatubo who has shaped with promise when third on both his starts this season, not enjoying a clear passage when third of 14 in 5f Beverley handicap 27 days ago; a likely player off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Not smooth run when promising third at Beverley latest; likely player off the same mark

runners.name: Mighty Magnus
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when second on reappearance at Ripon (6f) in April but he beat only one in 6f Hamilton handicap the following month; this fair 5.5f juvenile winner shouldn't be written off.
runners.comment: Beat one at Hamilton latest but this fair 5.5f juvenile winner shouldn't be written off

runners.name: Ambishio
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win at Southwell (5f) in January and in good form until only eighth in 5f Chester handicap 23 days ago; he's the sort to bounce back.
runners.comment: In good form until only eighth in Chester h'cap 23 days ago; he's the sort to bounce back

runners.name: Here Forever
runners.spotlight: Son of Havana Grey who improved with each of his three runs at two, landing 6f Ripon maiden in September on his final run for George Boughey; has since changed hands for 52,000gns (also gelded) and very much one to consider on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won at Ripon on final run at two for George Boughey; gelded/one to consider on h'cap debut

runners.name: Sands Of Josepi
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win in 5f Southwell novice in November but has failed to kick on, hooded when a hanging sixth of eight in 5f Haydock handicap on his return eight days ago; more is needed.
runners.comment: Gained a first win in Southwell novice in November but has failed to kick on; more needed

runners.name: Dazzling Haze
runners.spotlight: A dual 5f winner for Simon and Ed Crisford at two; made an encouraging start for new yard after seven months off when fifth of 14 over C&D last month so enters calculations.
runners.comment: Encouraging start for new yard with C&D fifth last month so enters calculations

runners.name: Straight Ahead
runners.spotlight: Improved for being gelded and bagged a pair of 5f nurseries at Newcastle late in 2025; back from five months off here but he's still not out of things nudged up 2lb.
runners.comment: Bagged two 5f nurseries at Newcastle late in 2025; off five months but not out of things

runners.name: Go Vince Go
runners.spotlight: A three-time juvenile winner (including 5f York nursery); better form in defeat this season, prominent for a long way when a very good third of 14 in 6f handicap at Hamilton two weeks ago; interesting reverting to 5f.
runners.comment: Very good third in h'cap at Hamilton two weeks ago; interesting reverted to minimum trip

runners.name: Under The Radar
runners.spotlight: Landed C&D maiden last May and largely performed with credit after; shaped as if the run was needed after nine months off when fifth of eight at Haydock (5f) on his reappearance and he should strip sharper here.
runners.comment: C&D winner; off nine months before Haydock fifth on return; should strip sharper here

races.race_name: Sky Bet Club Handicap

runners.name: Mon Na Slieve
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent front-runner but was close up behind Vantheman at Hamilton and this flatter track could suit better; respected.
runners.comment: Close up behind Vantheman at Hamilton and this flatter track could suit better; respected

runners.name: American Bay
runners.spotlight: One of his two wins came over C&D and was a close fifth here a month ago, backing up two good runs on the AW; possibilities, especially should the new cheekpieces give him a lift.
runners.comment: In form, goes well at this track and the new cheekpieces could give him a lift

runners.name: Muker
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner, including here; quiet this season but it was the same last campaign before he won on his third outing; handy mark.
runners.comment: Won third time out last season after a quiet beginning so he could be interesting

runners.name: Acrisius
runners.spotlight: Won off 4lb lower at Chester (5f, good) last July and perhaps unsuited by softer going in two starts since; only had 12 races and couldn't dismiss.
runners.comment: Chester winner last July and perhaps unsuited by softer going in two starts since

runners.name: Montezuma
runners.spotlight: Competitive over 5f at Musselburgh this spring and wasn't at his best at Hamilton two weeks ago (6f); capable of bouncing back.
runners.comment: Below best at Hamilton recently but bang in form prior to that and could bounce back
runners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

runners.name: Equity Law
runners.spotlight: Useful mark and was bang there in Kinetic Force's race here earlier this month; below that level back here 15 days ago but again he travelled well for a long way and he's still in good form.
runners.comment: Again travelled strongly when sixth here 15 days ago and remains in good form

runners.name: Al Hussar
runners.spotlight: Running well over 5f before another respectable show over 6f at York last week; little margin for error off this mark but remains in good nick.
runners.comment: Little margin for error off this mark but remains in good nick

runners.name: Vantheman
runners.spotlight: None too consistent but he surged back to form with a win at Hamilton (5f, good; made all) two weeks ago; that was only his second go in blinkers and he's again of interest off 4lb higher.
runners.comment: Only his second run in blinkers when winning at Hamilton; of interest off 4lb higher

runners.name: I'm Next
runners.spotlight: Dropped away in a Class 2 at York last time but had previously looked a 4yo on the upgrade and remains on a competitive mark; makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: Dropped away in a Class 2 at York last time but had previously looked a 4yo on the upgrade

runners.name: Kinetic Force
runners.spotlight: Has made a good start for Ruth Carr and emerged from midfield to edge home over C&D a month ago; on that run and past exploits, he remains on a useful mark up 4lb.
runners.comment: Recent C&D winner; on that run and past exploits, he remains on a useful mark up 4lb

runners.name: Copper Knight
runners.spotlight: Grand old veteran who can be forgiven his defeat in a Class 2 at York last time, having won at this level the time before at Beverley (2lb lower); had 119 races and yet this is his first visit here.
runners.comment: Won at this level at Beverley before flying too high in a Class 2 at York

runners.name: Vince L'Amour
runners.spotlight: Recent Ripon winner but poor on either side of that and he has become hard to predict.
runners.comment: Recent Ripon winner but poor on either side of that and he has become hard to predict

runners.name: Far Above The Law
runners.spotlight: Did well over 6f for Alice Haynes and coped fine with the drop back to 5f at Ascot where he was prominent throughout and prevailed by just over a length; this 6lb higher mark isn't beyond him.
runners.comment: Prominent throughout when winning at Ascot (5f); this higher mark isn't beyond him

runners.name: Woohoo
runners.spotlight: Fifth career win when beating seven rivals at Musselburgh (5f, good) and she got behind before staying on in Vantheman's race at Hamilton; drawn on the wing but has a big run in her.
runners.comment: Got behind before staying on in Vantheman's race at Hamilton

runners.name: Spring Is Sprung
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 8-34 includes five turf wins; seventh behind Vantheman at Hamilton 14 days ago but moved well for a long way before fading late on.
runners.comment: Seventh behind Vantheman at Hamilton 14 days ago but moved well for a long way

runners.name: King's Call
runners.spotlight: Eighth of 24 in a 5f handicap at Royal Ascot last season but he's finished out the back in his subsequent runs; bit risky.
runners.comment: Essentially disappointing since running surprisingly well at last season's Royal Ascot

runners.name: Changeofmind
runners.spotlight: Up in grade after winning a Class 5 at Pontefract (5f, good to soft) but had dropped to a tempting mark; wouldn't count the 6lb rise against him.
runners.comment: Pontefract winner last week; wouldn't count the 6lb rise against him

runners.name: Mondammej
runners.spotlight: Veteran with a mind of his own and refused to leave the parade ring on last intended start in November; however, perfectly capable of a big run off this mark and he's gone well fresh in the past.
runners.comment: Quirky but capable of a big run off this mark and he's gone well fresh in the past

races.race_name: Sky Bet Extra Places Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Great Bedwyn
runners.spotlight: Beaten on his last 13 starts going back to last May but he's been a good second on two of his last three starts, most recently in a big field at York (1m4f, good) this month; every chance he'll again be bang there.
runners.comment: Runner-up in big field at York this month and every chance he'll again be bang there

runners.name: Fast Fred
runners.spotlight: Disappointing behind Letsbefrank at Hamilton a fortnight ago but in good form otherwise this year, with two close seconds sandwiching a win at Musselburgh (1m4f, good); no surprise if he bounces back with a big performance.
runners.comment: Disappointing at Hamilton recently but in good form otherwise this year; could bounce back

runners.name: Damascus Steel
runners.spotlight: Two 1m4f AW wins last winter for Denis Quinn and respectable fifth of 15 at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) last month on sole run for John Ryan; however, it's a long time since he ran well on turf and has something to prove back on grass; stable debut.
runners.comment: In good form on AW over the last few months but a long time since he ran well on turf

runners.name: Valley Of Flowers
runners.spotlight: Consistent last July-September and nicely treated on that form; however, she hasn't been at the same level on her two outings this year.
runners.comment: Nicely treated on form last July-September but not at same level on her two runs this year

runners.name: Letsbefrank
runners.spotlight: He's reappeared in rude health this year with wins under Lauren Young at Musselburgh (1m4f, good) and Hamilton (1m3f, good); beat bang-in-form stablemate last time and a 5lb higher mark might not stop this course winner.
runners.comment: 2-2 this season and 5lb rise for latest win might not stop this thriving 6yo

runners.name: Eroico
runners.spotlight: Ran in five maidens in Ireland for Noel Meade, finishing second on the final occasion (1m4f, heavy); has also shown promise on good ground; she could improve for last month's stable/seasonal debut at Musselburgh (1m4f, good) but was soundly beaten and others have more pressing claims; a hood goes on.
runners.comment: Unexposed and could improve for last month's stable debut but she very much needs to

runners.name: Serenity Blue
runners.spotlight: Two novice wins last May/June (1m2f, good to firm) and back on track when third in handicap at Kempton (1m4f, AW) last month; down the field at Ascot (1m4f, good to firm) since on first run since being gelded but this 4yo remains lightly raced and is not written off.
runners.comment: Down the field at Ascot on first run since gelded but lightly raced and not written off

runners.name: Optician
runners.spotlight: 0-14 on turf (5-19 on AW) but he ran well when he last tackled grass in summer 2024; returned from some time off with promising fourth at Southwell (1m3f, AW) last month and can be in the mix if building on that effort.
runners.comment: 0-14 on turf but ran well when last tackling grass; promising return to action last month

runners.name: Penzance
runners.spotlight: Having dropped down the weights he returned to form when runner-up off today's mark at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) recently on his second start for this yard; however, he's 5-22 on AW as opposed to 0-11 on Flat turf, and needs to back up that latest performance now back on grass.
runners.comment: Returned to form when second on AW recently but has to back that up returned to turf

runners.name: Spanish Hustle
runners.spotlight: Hamilton specialist who was tailed off there on his reappearance a fortnight ago (pulled hard); hard to fancy on the back of that but it may have got the freshness out of him and he's on his last winning mark; not a forlorn hope on his first visit here.
runners.comment: Pulled hard when tailed off on reappearance; that might have got the freshness out of him

runners.name: Brielle
runners.spotlight: Won a novice at Hamilton (1m, good) last July and back on the scoresheet at Ayr (1m2f, good to soft) in September on third handicap start; failed to kick on subsequently but had excuses for those two runs; there can be be optimism that 1m4f will suit; could go well in first-time tongue-tie on return.
runners.comment: Excuses on final two starts last term; this lightly raced 4yo could go well on her return

runners.name: Cabrera
runners.spotlight: Ran just once last year (last April) and also unable to pose a threat on her comeback at Newcastle this March; however, she's on a handy mark on her 2024 exploits and may be able to rediscover that form at some point.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced since 2024; may be able to rediscover her peak form at some point
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing 1 And Mrs David Egan

races.race_name: Sky Bet Price Boosts Handicap

runners.name: Sir Yoshi
runners.spotlight: He's on a competitive mark on last season's best form for David Marnane but he was well beaten on his stable debut at York last October and it was the same outcome there this month on his return; he has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Competitive mark but well beaten on final run last year and same outcome on reappearance

runners.name: Fandom
runners.spotlight: Globetrotter who has had mixed fortunes across his career but he was back on song when third of 22 to Dark Thirty at York (6f, good) this month on his second stable start; he can be in the mix if confirming that promise.
runners.comment: Back on song when third in big field at York and in the mix if confirming that promise

runners.name: Gangsta Man
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Newmarket last month (7f, good; has won at that trip in the past) but he failed to settle and didn't look at home on the track; prior to that he kept on strongly to go very close at Musselburgh (5f, soft) on his stable debut and he remains one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Went close at Musselburgh on his stable debut and had excuses at Newmarket last time

runners.name: Russet Gold
runners.spotlight: Beaten on his last 20 starts going back to June 2023 but he's shown promise in fifth this spring on his first two starts for this yard, most recently behind a couple of these in a big field at York (6f, good); he continues to edge down the weights and might not be far away.
runners.comment: Losing run continues but he's shown promise on both runs for new yard this spring

runners.name: Dark Thirty
runners.spotlight: Returned to form to win big-field handicap at York (6f, good) this month on third stable start, going one better than in the same race 12 months previously; a 6lb rise shouldn't be enough to prevent another big run.
runners.comment: Returned to form to win in big field at York; 6lb rise shouldn't prevent another big run

runners.name: Yes I'm Mali
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) last month on second stable start; midfield finish at Newbury (6f, good) next time before 1l second of 20 at York (6f, good) last Saturday; he could still have more to offer for this yard; respected.
runners.comment: Close 2nd at York last Saturday; could still have more to offer for this yard; respected

runners.name: Fluorescence
runners.spotlight: Made it three wins from his five starts when landing a handicap at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last August; her two other wins came at 6f; returned to form when staying on for fourth of 14 at Southwell (5f, AW) last September when last seen and the return to 6f could be a good move on her comeback.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo for whom the return to 6f could be a good move on her reappearance

runners.name: Strike Red
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten behind Dark Thirty and others at York (6f, good) last time but his high draw proved to be a negative; won at Leicester (6f, good to firm) two starts ago and he could bounce back today and be involved.
runners.comment: Won at Leicester last month and a high draw proved to be a negative at York last time

runners.name: Toyotomi
runners.spotlight: Having been gelded he made a promising reappearance at Newcastle (6f, AW) last month; failed to build on that when sixth at Ascot (6f, good) but he got upset in the stalls; well handicapped on his French form up to last July and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Promising reappearance at Newcastle last month; upset in stalls when below par since

runners.name: Milford
runners.spotlight: 0-14 since debut win in May 2024 but he often ran well in some quality handicaps last season; returns on a competitive mark having had wind surgery; could have a part to play if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: 0-14 since debut win in May 2024 but often runs well and he could have a part to play

runners.name: Annaf
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner at Ascot (6f, soft) last October and he's versatile ground-wise; down to a dangerous mark but he hasn't been at the top of his game on his four runs this year, in handicaps on the last two occasions, and he needs to turn things around in a first-time tongue-tie and reapplied cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner at Ascot last October but he hasn't been at the top of his game this season

runners.name: Manila Scouse
runners.spotlight: His last win came over C&D last July (good; off 1lb higher) and he stayed on for a close third at Hamilton (5f, good) a fortnight ago; could build on that latest performance now back up in trip; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: C&D winner who stayed on for close 3rd over 5f at Hamilton latest; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Eternal Sunshine
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick last August/September and she's capable of going close off her current mark if at her best; she's 3-3 here and perhaps the return to Thirsk will trigger a revival, but it very much needs to as she's not shone on her three outings this season.
runners.comment: Completed hat-trick last August/September but soundly beaten on her three runs this season

runners.name: Al Shabab Storm
runners.spotlight: Finished down the field on stable/seasonal debut at Chester (7.5f) this month but that doesn't tell the full story as he ran well for a long way from his wide draw and was hampered late on; he's dropped to an attractive mark and is just the type his new trainer does very well with.
runners.comment: Ran better than finishing position suggests at Chester on stable debut; well handicapped

races.race_name: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: St Faz
runners.spotlight: Usually races prominently; won four of his six starts for new yard this year, including when returned to turf for eight-runner race at Musselburgh (2m, good) in April; has excuses for the two defeats, on latest occasion fifth of 17 in the 2m2f Chester Plate despite the work he had to do to front-run from wide draw; one to note.
runners.comment: Won four of six starts for yard; excuses for the two defeats, fifth in the Chester Plate

runners.name: Artisan Dancer
runners.spotlight: Industrious and dependable sort at 1m6f-2m2f but turf strike-rate is 1-19; not discredited on latest start (1m5f) but others are preferred.
runners.comment: Industrious and dependable sort at 1m6f-2m2f but his turf strike-rate is 1-19

runners.name: Almuhit
runners.spotlight: Rediscovered the knack for new stable last year, including two front-running wins over 2m in four-runner Flat races and two places when the action got more competitive; returned to some hurdles form on last two outings in that sphere; a lesser show on Musselburgh Flat in between was over 1m6f but this race may also prove too tough.
runners.comment: Two front-running wins over 2m in four-runner Flat races last year; back up from 1m6f

runners.name: Pole Star
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m6f handicaps last term; back-to-form third with a first-time visor at Newmarket (1m6f) four weeks ago, so he re-enters calculations despite earlier lesser efforts over 2m2f and 2m.
runners.comment: Back-to-form third with a first-time visor (1m6f) following lesser efforts over 2m2f/2m
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Treasure Islands
runners.spotlight: Improved for the step up in trip when making all at Ripon (2m, good) late last month on his second handicap start; not discredited when second on soft ground at Haydock (2m) ten days ago; needs to resume progress but that might be possible for this lightly raced 4yo.
runners.comment: Needs to resume progress (2nd on soft latest); it is possible for this lightly raced 4yo

runners.name: New York Minute
runners.spotlight: Headgear helped on AW last September-December, including for 1m6f/2m hat-trick; peak form was not repeated this year, including in two small fields this spring but they were at 1m3f/1m4f; returning to 2m should be a big help but none of his turf form (five races) approaches this mark.
runners.comment: Return to 2m should be a big help but none of his turf form (5 races) approaches this mark

runners.name: Arrange
runners.spotlight: Last won in August 2024; placed in three of eight starts last term and it was a hot race in which she was well beaten at York (40-1) on 2026 return; 15lb below peak mark but others seem to be banging on the door more loudly.
runners.comment: 15lb below peak mark but others seem to be banging on the door more loudly
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs G F Hall Racing

runners.name: Mountain Road
runners.spotlight: 4-15 for David Simcock; four runs for current yard, absent 582 days before this season's, in which he showed that significant ability remains; needs to build on those performances but going back up from the 1m5f of last two outings should suit and he continues down the weights; case can be made.
runners.comment: Going back up from the 1m5f of last two runs should suit and he continues down the weights

runners.name: Anniversary
runners.spotlight: Blinkered on four of his last five starts; flopped when 5-4 favourite behind Elysian Flame at Beverley (2m, good to soft) in April but this month's second of nine at Salisbury (1m6f, good; made most and rallied well) was his closest finish since 2yo and gives some renewed hope.
runners.comment: Rallying second at Salisbury (1m6f, good; made most) latest start gives some renewed hope

runners.name: Minstrel Knight
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably for minor honours in two (1m5f/2m2f) of his four starts in last year's Flat campaign and won novice hurdles in December and January when last seen; however, although he won on good ground two years ago, his record since has been dominated by achievements on softer.
runners.comment: Won on good two years ago but record since has been dominated by achievements on softer

runners.name: Gibside
runners.spotlight: Strong finish from rear secured the Chester Plate (2m2f, good) last May off 3lb higher and he also won over 1m4f on soft at Catterick in October; needs to leave his four efforts this season a long way behind him, however.
runners.comment: On a good mark but needs to leave his four efforts this season a long way behind him

runners.name: Elysian Flame
runners.spotlight: Smart staying handicapper for Mick and David Easterby in 2021 when competitive at some of the top meetings; only his second run since when winning over 2m at Beverley (good to soft; 2lb higher today) in April, while 1m5f helps to explain his last of five at Hamilton four weeks ago; not dismissed.
runners.comment: 2m win at Beverley (good to soft; 2lb higher today) in April; just 1m5f for sole run since

runners.name: Laafy
runners.spotlight: Formerly with Sir Michael Stoute, smart over 1m4f in 2020 when usually visored; absent for 1373 days before last season and staged a partial recovery when tried in cheekpieces at Musselburgh (1m6f) on second run back, admittedly in only eighth of 14 (beaten about 5l); dropped another 6lb since but also absent another 407 days.
runners.comment: Absent for 1373 days before last season; partial recovery last April but off again since

races.race_name: Sky Bet Build A Bet Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Thaluna
runners.spotlight: Won 2yo novice at York (6f, good) last October on second start; mid-division in Listed race later that month and reappeared with good second on handicap debut at Newmarket (7f, good to firm); stayed on well last time and this step up to 1m could prompt further improvement; lots to like.
runners.comment: Second on handicap debut at Newmarket; the step up to 1m could prompt further improvement

runners.name: Bintaziza
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in April and again had something to spare when following up at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW); she has the potential to be better than her opening mark and is one to consider.
runners.comment: 2-2 and has the potential to be better than her opening mark; she's one to consider

runners.name: Mandarin Spirit
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden here (7f, good) last July on second start; well beaten in Group 2 next time; she could improve for this month's reappearance sixth on handicap debut at Beverley (7.4f, good to soft) but her claims aren't compelling on the back of that.
runners.comment: Could improve for handicap debut sixth but claims aren't compelling on the back of that

runners.name: Geo
runners.spotlight: Exploited a reduced mark when returning to form with a narrow win at Ayr (1m, good to firm) 11 days ago; this is more competitive but she's gone back up just 2lb and is not discounted.
runners.comment: Narrowly exploited a reduced mark at Ayr; this is more competitive but she's up just 2lb

runners.name: Annastarzy
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo who appears to be on a fair mark judged on her novice form; still early days in handicaps but she was merely eighth of 12 at Windsor (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago on her first attempt in this sphere.
runners.comment: Promise in novice races but merely eighth of 12 on handicap debut at Windsor

runners.name: Krissy
runners.spotlight: Easily made all at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) ten days ago and did likewise under a 5lb penalty in apprentice handicap at Carlisle (1m1f, good) on Friday; unpenalised for latest win but new mark has kicked in, which is 6lb higher than that which she raced off two days ago; in a tougher race today but clearly thriving and she's a leading candidate if her fine form continues.
runners.comment: Easily made all Carlisle on Friday; 6lb higher today but still a leading candidate

runners.name: Thankfully
runners.spotlight: Promising third at Sandown (7f, soft) last August on the first of her two 2yo starts and reappeared with win in a maiden at Beverley last month (8.4f, good to soft; unraced on softer); she has a useful pedigree and is in good hands to progress; possible player on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won Beverley maiden last month and this 3yo is in good hands to progress
runners.owner: Mrs Tabor, Mrs Mv Magnier, Mrs Shanahan

runners.name: Bridget's View
runners.spotlight: Won at Southwell (1m, AW) in December and very close fifth at Lingfield (7f, AW) in February; however, down the field on both starts since (7f, AW/good) and needs to leave those performances well behind.
runners.comment: Ran well as recently as February but down the field on both starts since

runners.name: Perfect Part
runners.spotlight: She's been a promising fourth on two of her three starts this season, at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) in March and here (7f, good) a fortnight ago in first-time cheekpieces (retained); might not be far away.
runners.comment: Promising fourth on two of her three starts this season; might not be far away

runners.name: Renewal
runners.spotlight: Record of 4232 in novice/maiden events on turf and again ran well when runner-up on handicap debut at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) last October; shaped on that latest outing as though the drop back in trip may be a positive and this unexposed 4yo could have a big part to play on her reappearance.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo who could have a big part to play back down to 1m on her reappearance

runners.name: Wetsand
runners.spotlight: 1m 2yo winner last August and the return to this trip could help; kept on for fifth of eight at Yarmouth (7f, good to firm) last month, which was a step back in the right direction, but she needs another today.
runners.comment: Yarmouth last time was a step back in the right direction but she needs another today

races.race_name: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Ischgl
runners.spotlight: Belied odds of 100-1 to chase home Ballydoyle hotpot and subsequent Group 3 winner on Curragh debut (6f, soft); confirmed that wasn't a fluke when taking a close fourth back at the Curragh (6f, good) earlier this month; should run his race once again but could be vulnerable to more talented types.
runners.comment: Belied 100-1 odds when runner-up on debut and took close fourth last time; solid.

runners.name: The Scallionator
runners.spotlight: Improved markedly from debut when stepped up to 6f at Navan and keeping on well for second (good); 7l clear of the rest on that occasion; that run puts him on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Improved markedly over debut when runner-up at Navan, 7l clear of remainder; contender

runners.name: Glenade
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear colt did well to finish as close as he did given the strong pace he set on debut at Roscommon when third (7.5f, good to yielding); may settle better with that run under his belt and the drop in trip can help too; big chance.
runners.comment: Did well to finish as close as he did on debut when keen; big chance down in trip

runners.name: Hip Hop Chrissy
runners.spotlight: 45,000euros yearling; first foal; dam Listed-placed 6f/7f winner (inc AW; RPR 100), half-sister to winners Faraasah (6f) and Felippo (German/French 7f-12.5f), out of unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Muthabara; second of ten in a Naas barrier trial recently.
runners.comment: 45,000euros yearling; dam Listed-placed 6f/7f winner; 2nd of ten in recent barrier trial
runners.owner: Eclipse T'bred Partners/Gaynor Bloodstock LLC/F Gallagher

runners.name: Kraar
runners.spotlight: E6,000 foal and 15,000euros yearling; first foal; dam 1m winner (RPR 64), half-sister to winners Cunning Fox (French/Polish 6f-1m3f) and Investisseur (French 6f-7.5f), out of Group-placed 7f/1m 2yo winner; trainer's other runner looks more appealing at this preliminary stage.
runners.comment: 15,000euros yearling; dam 1m winner (RPR 64); yard's other runner looks more appealing

runners.name: Shesthedevil
runners.spotlight: Cotai Glory filly; dam 7f winner (RPR 83), sister to winners Mr Rumbalicious (2m Flat/2m1f hurdle) and Eye For An Eye (Australian 8.5f), out of unraced sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner and Derby runner-up Libertarian; hooded for debut and likely best watched.
runners.comment: Cotai Glory filly; dam 7f winner (RPR 83); hooded for debut and likely best watched

runners.name: Tina Tereshkova
runners.spotlight: Unraced dam closely related to winners Tariq (7f Group 2) and Tariq Too (useful 6.5f/7f), out of unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Trimming; unlikely to make immediate impact; hood on for debut.
runners.comment: Unraced dam closely related to two winners; unlikely to make immediate impact

runners.name: Chico Guapo
runners.spotlight: Magna Grecia colt; half-brother to winners Paddy's Day (5f/6f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 95), Chica Guerrera (7f 2yo/1m4f; 81) and Shifting Gold (1m-1m4f; 74); dam 6f AW winner (71), half-sister to 1m Group 2 winner Duntle; others preferred.
runners.comment: Half-brother to three winners inc' Paddy's Day (RPR 95); others preferred

runners.name: No Time Faults
runners.spotlight: Beaten 10l on debut at Naas over 5f; a lot of improvement required.
runners.comment: Beaten 10l on debut at Naas over 5f; a lot of improvement required

runners.name: Belicoso
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; finished a short head in front of High Morals on debut at the Curragh (6f, good), but 4.25l behind Ischgl; likely to improve over that first run, but needs to.
runners.comment: Barely in front of High Morals on debut but 4.25l behind Ischgl; likely to improve

runners.name: The Drambling Man
runners.spotlight: First reserve; beaten 8.5l on debut over 5f at Cork on soft, hanging right; others preferred.
runners.comment: Reserve; beaten 8.5l on debut over 5f at Cork on soft, hanging right; others preferred

runners.name: Eighteenth Smiles
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; just a respectable fifth of ten at Cork (5f, soft) and filled the same spot at Redcar upped to 6f last time (good); likely to improve over further in the future.
runners.comment: Okay fifth on debut and filled same spot latest; likely to improve over further in future

runners.name: Portaliffe Phil
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; beat just one rival over 6f at Cork; unlikely.
runners.comment: Reserve; beat just one rival over 6f at Cork; unlikely

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Big Negotiator
runners.spotlight: Havana Grey filly was a warm order for her debut at Navan over 5f on yielding when outpaced late on to finish third; improvement likely upped in trip and has race experience; big player.
runners.comment: Warrm order for her debut over 5f when outpaced and finishing third; big player

runners.name: Beibhinn
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; weakened into fourth of six in what looks like a strong Naas maiden (6f, good); should improve for that and no surprise to see her in the mix.
runners.comment: Weakened into fourth in a strong m'den on debut; no surprise to see her in the mix

runners.name: Sister Nancy
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; Masar filly; 22,000euros yearling; half-sister to winners La Pasionaria (Listed-placed 1m 2yo; RPR 100) and Artaois (7.5f/1m inc 2yo/Singapore; 84); dam French Listed-placed 8.5f AW winner (91), half-sister to 1m2f 2yo Listed winner Empire Day; hooded for debut.
runners.comment: 22,000euros yearling; half-sister to two winners inc' Listed placed La Pasionaria; hooded

runners.name: Citrus Springs
runners.spotlight: Olympiad filly cost 65,000USD as a yearling and 300,000USD at the breeze-ups; seventh foal; half-sister to US winners Spirit Maker (1m-1m1f turf inc 2yo) and Not Guilty (5f AW); dam once-raced half-sister to US 8.5f Grade 2 winner Protonico, is granddaughter of US 8.5f Grade 1 winner Wild Spirit; very interesting debutante.
runners.comment: Cost 300,000USD at the breeze-ups; half-sister to two US winners; interesting debutante

runners.name: Exchange Student
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Iko Iko (US stakes-placed 1m turf), Roguery (Australia 1m2f) and Albanian Lady (Australian 6.5f); dam twice-raced half-sister to US 1m2f Grade 1 winner Nereid; check market.
runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners abroad inc' US stakes-placed Iko Iko; check market

runners.name: Damsel In Distress
runners.spotlight: 26,000gns foal and 25,000gns yearling; half-sister to 11.4f winner Schemaya (RPR 83); dam French Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner (91), half-sister to French Group-placed 1m winner Like A Charm and German 7f Listed winner Shehna; market can split trainer's two.
runners.comment: 25,000gns yearling; half-sister to winner Schemaya (RPR 83); market to split trainer's two

runners.name: Madam Secretary
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly; sister to 7f 2yo winner Amadeus Mozart (RPR 96); dam once-raced sister to winners Zagitova (Group-placed 1m 2yo) and Frill (7f), half-sister to 6.5f Group 1 winner Garswood, out of unraced close relative of 7f Group 3 winner Infallible; of obvious interest.
runners.comment: Wootton Bassett filly is a sister to 7f 2yo winner Amadeus Mozart; of obvious interest
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Stellar Path
runners.spotlight: Beat just one rival at Cork on debut over 5f on soft; unlikely.
runners.comment: Beat just one rival at Cork on debut over 5f on soft; unlikely

races.race_name: Croom House Stud Handicap

runners.name: Apache Outlaw
runners.spotlight: Last win was in late 2024, but was second over 7f at last year's Galway festival and some good AW runs over the winter; has much lower mark on turf.
runners.comment: Long time winless but some good AW runs over the winter and much lower mark on turf

runners.name: Mickey The Steel
runners.spotlight: First reserve; five-time winner loves the mud and solid placed form over 6f/7f early in the season; has had a break since unplaced run at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Loves the mud and solid placed form over 6f/7f early in the season; reserve

runners.name: Ribble Emperor
runners.spotlight: Can lead and some fair placed form in non-handicaps over 7f/1m, including when outclassed in third behind easy winner in AW maiden on Tuesday; mark probably stiff enough.
runners.comment: Can lead and some fair placed form over 7f/1m; mark probably stiff enough

runners.name: Altimara
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back 7f wins at Leopardstown in August and placed twice over 1m subsequently; this trip looks too sharp.
runners.comment: Back-to-back 7f wins at Leopardstown in August and placed twice over 1m after

runners.name: Unauthorized
runners.spotlight: Front-runner won 7f Leopardstown maiden at two and respectable effort on comeback in 3yo handicap at the Curragh, weakening late over 7f; down in grade and good apprentice on board.
runners.comment: Front-runner won 7f maiden at two and respectable effort on comeback at the Curragh

runners.name: Felix Somary
runners.spotlight: Has been disappointing since debut win over this trip at Sligo, not seeing out his races; drops back in distance and headgear removed.
runners.comment: Has been disappointing since debut win over this trip at Sligo; drops back in distance

runners.name: Dragon Of Malta
runners.spotlight: Course specialist won on successive days at last September's festival (7f/1m on heavy); well beaten on return; opposable at present.
runners.comment: Course specialist won on successive days at last September's festival; well held on return

runners.name: Little Empire
runners.spotlight: Admirably consistent on AW and turf; close fourth over 7f at Musselburgh last month and probably best at that trip; stuck out wide, too.
runners.comment: Admirably consistent on AW and turf; close fourth over 7f at Musselburgh last month

runners.name: Mint Man
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f at Naas last summer and fair fourth over 5f back there latest; blinkers added and he's back on a winnable mark.
runners.comment: Won over 6f at Naas last summer and fair fourth over 5f back there latest; blinkers added

runners.name: Famed Again
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; maiden had fair 7f form at two for Tom Cooper but not much yet for current yard, including when mid-field over 7.5f on Roscommon return.
runners.comment: Fair form at two but not much last year and only mid-field over 7.5f on return; reserve

runners.name: Alfred Tennyson
runners.spotlight: Won 7f maiden for Ballydoyle last summer; best AW run came in March when beaten less than a length in third over 6f; missed break when well-held fourth at Navan last time.
runners.comment: Beaten less than a length when third on AW; missed break when well-held fourth at Navan

runners.name: Inishfallen
runners.spotlight: Proving hard to win with; not beaten far last two AW runs, behind I Bid You Ajou when sixth at Naas last time; handicapper starting to play ball.
runners.comment: Hard to win with; not beaten far last two AW runs, behind I Bid You Ajou at Naas latest
runners.owner: F J Carragher/Yorkavenue Syndicate/Park Ave Syndicate

runners.name: Mercurial
runners.spotlight: Over two years since latest of only two wins; second over 6f at the Curragh in October and ran well over 7.5f on return, but poor strike-rate overall; prefers it soft but handles good.
runners.comment: Second over 6f at the Curragh in October and ran well over 7.5f on return, but rarely wins

runners.name: I Bid You Ajou
runners.spotlight: Clicked last summer with a Fairyhouse hat-trick over 6f on nice ground; touched off at Naas two starts back and couldn't get in the clear until too late when beaten just over 2l in premier handicap at the Curragh last weekend.
runners.comment: Couldn't get in the clear until too late when beaten just over 2l at the Curragh last time

runners.name: Aurora Nova
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough over 6f, but her three wins last term were all over 5f, two of them at Bellewstown where she had a good comeback run in April.
runners.comment: Dual 5f winner at Bellewstown last term and good comeback run at same track in April

races.race_name: Coco At The Arcade Race

runners.name: Tim Toe
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner and fair maiden hurdler; won Bellewstown maiden over 1m3f; third in slowly-run 1m6f heat at Killarney and opening mark likely flatters him; surely beatable over this trip.
runners.comment: Form is over farther and opening mark likely flatters him; surely beatable over this trip

runners.name: Thread Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Bought by current owners after debut win at the Curragh (7f, good), the form of which is fairly solid; down the field in 1m1f Group 3 next time; market support would be reassuring.
runners.comment: Maiden winner over 7f on last year's debut, down the field in 1m1f Group 3 next time
runners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & Gary Barber

runners.name: Sursum Corda
runners.spotlight: Has kept good company in three starts (7f/1m) and initial mark looks fair, but that leaves her with a bit to find in this company, albeit the weight she gets would account for most of that on paper.
runners.comment: Has kept good company in three starts (7f/1m) and initial mark looks fair

runners.name: God Of Power
runners.spotlight: Handed mark of 80 after 7f maiden win at Limerick; open to improvement but stiff task here.
runners.comment: Handed mark of 80 after 7f maiden win at Limerick; open to improvement but stiff task here

runners.name: Satoyama
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; prominent throughout when readily beating a subsequent winner at Gowran (1m, good to yielding); looks a nice prospect.
runners.comment: Prominent throughout over 1m at Gowran when readily beating a subsequent winner

races.race_name: James Collins Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Linger For Longer
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner was below par last year and didn't provide much encouragement on her return at Limerick.
runners.comment: Below par last year and didn't provide much encouragement on her return

runners.name: The Blue Panther
runners.spotlight: Added his fifth career win with a heavy-ground C&D success in September off 6lb lower; a lot of improvement needed based on his return at Gowran in April.
runners.comment: Added fifth career win with a C&D victory in September; poor on return in April
runners.owner: Ms Julianne McNally & Colette McNally & D McNally

runners.name: Methgal
runners.spotlight: First reserve; made the breakthrough in a 7f Dundalk handicap in March and followed that with runner-up finishes over the same trip back at Dundalk and on turf at Leopardstown (yielding to soft); below his best at the Curragh last time.
runners.comment: Reserve; gained first win in March on AW and runner-up twice afterwards; below best latest

runners.name: Liamo
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-20 with the sole victory coming in this race last year on good ground off 7lb lower; consistent form on Flat over the autumn but his most recent maiden hurdle runs at huge odds were woeful; not discounted back on the level and his win came following a similar break.
runners.comment: Sole win came in this race last year off 7lb higher; not discounted

runners.name: Ukiyo
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f at Cork on yielding for Gavin Cromwell last year off 50; had Exceeding far behind when runner-up at Navan in March (6f, heavy); didn't back that up at Sligo; tongue tie goes on now.
runners.comment: Won over 6f at Cork last year; runner-up at Navan but didn't back it up at Sligo

runners.name: Exceeding
runners.spotlight: Beat Methgal by a short head at Leopardstown (7f, yielding to soft) to gain his second win from 29 starts; raised a fair 3lb for that but overall form suggests he's not certain to back it up.
runners.comment: Gained a second win from 29 starts last time; not certain to back it up on overall form

runners.name: Rough Diamond
runners.spotlight: Smart handicapper back in the day for Eddie Lynam; wasn't able to add to his tally of five wins when moving to Britain; operating at a much lower level nowadays, but not showing much for present trainer.
runners.comment: Smart handicapper back in the day but not showing much for present trainer

runners.name: James Henry
runners.spotlight: Better AW performer; improved for better ground when a never-nearer third of 19 in an apprentice handicap at Leopardstown (1m, good) when last seen around this time last year; best watched on return/stable debut.
runners.comment: Better AW performer and has an absence of over a year to overcome

runners.name: Zabriskie Point
runners.spotlight: Both wins came as a juvenile for Charlie Hills at 5f/7f on different ground types; has plunged in the ratings and took third (0.5l behind Exceeding) at Leopardstown (7f, yielding to soft) in April; unplaced favourite at Gowran next time, but took the runner-up spot at Roscommon (7.5f, good to yielding); in the mix.
runners.comment: Plunged in ratings since dual juvenile wins; placed twice recently; in the mix
runners.owner: B White & M W White & Ms H McGuinness & D Sheridan

runners.name: Crackles
runners.spotlight: Not much to shout about in form for previous trainers; tailed off on handicap debut at Navan (1m2f, yielding) and not much better in a Gowran maiden (1m2f, heavy); others preferred.
runners.comment: Not much to shout about in form for previous trainers; others preferred

runners.name: Rockbury Lad
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; won off 6lb higher at Bellewstown last year (1m, good); latest run at Leopardstown was not encouraging.
runners.comment: Reserve; won off 6lb higher last year; latest run was not encouraging
runners.owner: P E Ryan & J G Shanahan

runners.name: Notforalongtime
runners.spotlight: 9-32 at Dundalk, but just 1-28 on turf; followed up a third-place finish at Cork (1m, good to yielding) by filling the same spot at the Curragh (7f, good), hampered before running on late; on a nice mark if putting it all together.
runners.comment: Ten-time winner but just one on turf, although has taken third last twice; on a nice mark

runners.name: Toy Soldier
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; won a maiden and a claimer in Britain; poor form for new yard until 2l fourth of 15 at Killarney last time; attractive mark if back in form.
runners.comment: Reserve; dual British winner was poor for new yard until decent fourth last time

runners.name: Fleetfootsoldier
runners.spotlight: 1-12 on turf (4-18 on AW); makes stable debut following moderate run in a Dundalk claimer in February; others preferred.
runners.comment: Five-time winner (four on AW) makes stable debut following moderate run in claimer in Feb'

runners.name: La La Lucrative
runners.spotlight: A winner over 6f on heavy ground in Britain and took a Roscommon handicap off 10lb higher in May last year (7.5f, good); moderate form shown on AW when last seen in December.
runners.comment: Dual winner including off 10lb higher last year; moderate on AW when last seen in Dec'

runners.name: Secret Magician
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 3-51; improved upon poor recent efforts when encountering good ground at Killarney (1m), finishing a close third after not getting a clear run; up 2lb but remains on a nice mark if backing up his last run.
runners.comment: Didn't get a clear run when third last time; remains on a nice mark if backing it up

runners.name: Run Forrest Run
runners.spotlight: Knows how to win (6-44 on turf) but losing run stretches back to October 2024; in poor form on AW when last seen in December; best watched on debut for new yard after break.
runners.comment: 6-44 on turf but nothing since October 2024; in poor for on AW when last seen

races.race_name: Lane Family Memorial Handicap

runners.name: Lagoon Nebula
runners.spotlight: Some good runs in defeat at up to 2m last season; moderate efforts over obstacles were punctuated by an okay fifth of 12 at Bellewstown (1m4f, soft) in April; tailed off at Downpatrick over hurdles last time; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Some good runs in defeat at up to 2m last season; okay fifth on Flat penultimately

runners.name: All In Peder
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back Flat winner in 2023, including this race off 2lb lower on good; tailed off over hurdles when last seen over a year ago.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2023; tailed off over hurdles when last seen over a year ago

runners.name: Desert Friend
runners.spotlight: Veteran has done a bit of everything and recorded his ninth win when easily scoring over 1m6f at Sligo last time (yielding to soft), benefitting from a truly-run race; up 8lb but in the mix.
runners.comment: Veteran recorded ninth win at Sligo last time; up 8lb but in the mix.

runners.name: Eben Zaabeel
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden in Britain for Charlie Hills, runner-up in AW handicaps final two starts in 2024; yet to match that form in Ireland; the tongue tie is new.
runners.comment: Seven-race m'den in Britain although placed twice; yet to match that form in Ireland

runners.name: Ballyadam
runners.spotlight: Good quality NH performer, including when good fourth in BetMGM Handicap Hurdle at Cheltenham; took the runner-up spot in a Ballinrobe maiden (1m5f, good to yielding) last time when 7l behind the winner; must carry top weight for Flat handicap debut but big chance.
runners.comment: Quality NH performer took runner-up spot in a m'den last time; big chance but top weight

runners.name: Baila Conmigo
runners.spotlight: Dual winner on the AW at 10.5f-1m4f in February/March; put a poor Bellewstown run behind him when taking the runner-up spot at Leopardstown (1m1f, yielding to soft) last time; unproven at this trip.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner took runner-up spot at Leopardstown latest; unproven at this trip

runners.name: Miss Gitana
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins, twice in Britain and over this trip at Tramore last July for Gordon Elliott; poor in two runs for new yard; tongue tie goes on but others preferred.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner inc' over this trip in July; poor in two runs for new yard

runners.name: Cala Gracioneta
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden has come up short in first three AW handicaps at 10.5f-1.4f; failed to beat a rival when last seen in January; hood goes on now upped in trip but others more likely.
runners.comment: Six-race m'den has come up short in three AW h'caps; others more likely

runners.name: Mystic Rose
runners.spotlight: Overall form not encouraging; didn't back up okay Bellewstown fourth at Gowran when well beaten; tries this new trip and pedigree doesn't provide too much hope.
runners.comment: Overall form not too encouraging; tries this new trip but pedigree doesn't give much hope

runners.name: Sonic Invader
runners.spotlight: Appreciated the step up to 1m6f at Gowran (good to yielding) for his second handicap start when keeping on for third; likely to be suited by this trip on that evidence and Keane remains aboard; big player.
runners.comment: Appreciated step up to 1m6f last time when a staying-on third; Keane remains aboard

runners.name: Edge Of Darkness
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over 1m4f back in the day in Britain; nothing like that sort of form for current yard, mark rapidly descending of late but 5lb outside the handicap here; declared reserve in 7.25 at Down Royal on Friday.
runners.comment: Four-time winner in Britain but nothing like that form for current yard; 5lb wrong

races.race_name: Acro Fire & Safety (Q.R.) Race

runners.name: Sky Captain
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 5yo won ordinary 1m4f maiden on last summer's debut before good second in 1m6f contest at Galway festival; 6-5 fav, visor went on for Killarney return earlier this month, couldn't get by similarly-rated rival who dictated a steady pace over 1m6f.
runners.comment: Lightly-raced 5yo looks worthy of his mark; second to  similarly-rated rival at Killarney

runners.name: Ndaawi
runners.spotlight: Smart dual-purposer type got the Galway Hurdle in the stewards' room last summer and beat very useful Absurde in conditions' hurdle last month; his Flat run at Chester in September proved that he's worthy of this mark and showed he had a bit of boot when running well over 1m on AW pre-Cheltenham.
runners.comment: Smart dual-purposer type won Cork conditions' hurdle last month; has solid Flat form

runners.name: The Mediator
runners.spotlight: Good dual-purpose performer and did well in amateur-rider contests in the autumn, winning over 2m at Clonmel and placed twice over 1m4f at the Curragh; fair bit to find with a couple of these; first ride for jockey.
runners.comment: Did well in amateur-rider contests in the autumn; fair bit to find with a couple of these
runners.owner: Mrs Sheila O'Ryan & John F Doyle & Grant Leon

runners.name: Act Of God
runners.spotlight: Old boy had a good career across the codes for the late Edward O'Grady; point-to-pointing lately and just here for the day out.
runners.comment: Old boy had a good career across the codes for the late Edward O'Grady; here for the spin

runners.name: Chain Of Destiny
runners.spotlight: Modest performer and only making up the numbers here.
runners.comment: Modest performer and only making up the numbers here

runners.name: Talking Tough
runners.spotlight: Modest performer and only making up the numbers here.
runners.comment: Modest performer and only making up the numbers here

races.race_name: Adare Manor Opportunity Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Gunnery Sergeant
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two maiden hurdles this year, although the value of the form is questionable; well beaten over fences on his last two starts, prominent before weakening from two out at this venue last time; definite chance in a race of this standard
runners.comment: Twice second in maiden hurdles, unconvincing form but has a definite chance in this grade

runners.name: Captain Hanley
runners.spotlight: Maiden on the Flat after 18 attempts, rated a lowly 51; now looks a different proposition after a promising introduction to hurdling at Clonmel; despite suffering interference, stayed on well over a similar trip to this, form strongly advertised by the winner Minella Diamond in winning easily at Limerick on Thursday
runners.comment: Weak form on the Flat, vastly improved on hurdles debut and the form has been boosted

runners.name: Facile Ocean
runners.spotlight: Favourite for four-runner bumper on debut in sole start for Tom Mullins but was too keen and finished third; two moderate runs in bumpers for this stable before a much-improved display when second at this venue 16 days ago on his hurdling debut; jumping was fine, needs to settle better.
runners.comment: Ordinary bumper form, gave improved display when second at this venue on  hurdling debut

runners.name: Mighty Berkshire
