runners.spotlight: Third in a six-runner maiden hurdle over 3m1f at this venue last summer; two moderate runs over fences before last November down the field in a handicap hurdle at Limerick at Christmas and absent since.
runners.comment: Third in a six-runner maiden hurdle over 3m1f at this venue last year; lacks a recent run

runners.name: Allroadsleadtorome
runners.spotlight: 20-1 when fifth of six in a Naas bumper in February (1m7f, heavy); well supported in a good-ground bumper at Kelso last month, made the running to three furlongs down, faded into a moderate fifth.
runners.comment: Attracted support at Kelso on the second of two bumper runs, faded into a moderate fifth

runners.name: Unknown Fortune
runners.spotlight: 66-1 when tailed off on belated debut at Down Royal early this month; can be ruled out.
runners.comment: 66-1 when tailed off on belated debut at Down Royal early this month, can be ruled out

runners.name: L'As de Watt
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably behind odds-on favourite I'm Slippy when 66-1 second in a 2m Cork maiden hurdle last November; no impact over 2m4f at Killarney after a break.
runners.comment: Second at 66-1 in a 2m Cork maiden hurdle last November, no impact over 2m4f at Killarney

runners.name: The Red Lad
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unplaced Flat/hurdles. not much encouragement from the pedigree generally.
runners.comment: First foal. dam unplaced Flat/hurdles. not much encouragement from the pedigree generally

runners.name: Robindeepot
runners.spotlight: Dam unraced, out of unraced sister to useful bumper/hurdle/chase winner Kerada (seven wins for Nicky Henderson) and a French chase winner.
runners.comment: Dam unraced, out of unraced sister to useful bumper/hurdle/chase winner Kerada

runners.name: Sonna Lady
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unplaced in points/over hurdles, half-sister to a point winner, no appeal.
runners.comment: First foal, dam unplaced in points/over hurdles, half-sister to a point winner, no appeal

runners.name: Imarajan
runners.spotlight: 65-rated Flat maiden, stayed 1m6f, placed at the Curragh last October on his final start for the Hartys; remote fourth in a maiden hurdle at Wexford in April on first start for the stable; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: 65-rated Flat maiden, remote fourth in a maiden hurdle at Wexford in April on stable debut

runners.name: Mento
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to two French winners; has run in two bumpers; not a big price when finishing with only one behind him at Leopardstown in March; contested a strong event at the Punchestown festival, may do better over hurdles in time.
runners.comment: Has run in two bumpers, not much so far, may do better over hurdles in time

runners.name: Creevybeg
runners.spotlight: Completely tailed off first time out in a 2m4f maiden at Down Royal seven weeks ago.
runners.comment: Completely tailed off first time out in a 2m4f maiden at Down Royal seven weeks ago.

runners.name: Beacon Bypass
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a point, a bumper and two maiden hurdles starts; no apparent chance.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a point, a bumper and two maiden hurdles starts, no apparent chance

races.race_name: Gannon Eco Novice Hurdle

runners.name: Master Haku
runners.spotlight: Placed in first two maiden hurdles; off the mark at the third attempt here over 16.5f (gd-yld) in April; weakened after the third last when upped to 2m4f (gd-yld) in novice hurdle at Killarney 20 days ago; stamina query upped further in trip here.
runners.comment: Course maiden hurdle winner, 2m4f seemed to stretch latest, has to go further here

runners.name: Local News
runners.spotlight: Belied odds of 100-1 to make a winning start in a Cork mares' maiden hurdle (2m3f, good) three weeks ago, beating a 117-rated rival by a head; the third from that race has won since and there's a bit of stamina on the dam side of her pedigree so there's a decent chance she'll stay this longer trip; chance.
runners.comment: Shock maiden hurdle win on debut, form boosted, decent chance she'll stay this trip

runners.name: Red Or White
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles and rated 94; has shown ability in maiden hurdles but the closest she's got to the winner was when beaten 6.75l into third of 9 at Sligo (2m4f, good) last time; should be sharper for that first run back after a break but steps into the unknown here trip wise; others appeal more.
runners.comment: 0-7 and rated 94, not a bad run at Sligo latest, sharper now but this trip is an unknown

runners.name: Pets Georgina
runners.spotlight: Modest form so far in two bumpers and was hampered and unseated at the third on hurdles debut at Ballinrobe just six days ago when she was a 33-1 shot; still early days but hard to make a case for on form.
runners.comment: Modest bumper form, unseated early on hurdles debut latest, hard to make a case for

races.race_name: Joe Quinn Transport Longford Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Finnicky Filly
runners.spotlight: 1-22 hurdles record in a negative, unplaced twice since a pleasing C&D second in April; other seem more reliable.
runners.comment: 1-22 hurdles record in a negative, unplaced twice since a pleasing C&D second in April

runners.name: Blue Reed
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 22 starts over hurdles; hard to be confident against that background, although recent form is quite respectable.
runners.comment: Still a maiden after 22 starts over hurdles, hard to be confident against that background

runners.name: Sheer Raz
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on his last two starts, tongue-tie might help to produce some improvement; one of the more likely candidates.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on his last two starts, tongue-tie might help to produce some improvement

runners.name: Kalsman
runners.spotlight: Downpatrick winner in June 2024 on good ground; ran only once last year; showed the benefit of a comeback run when beating Justicialism by 3l at Down Royal last month; rider reported he hung badly left at Downpatrick last time; decent chance if he handles this track.
runners.comment: Disappointed at Downpatrick after beating today's rival Justicialism at Down Royal

runners.name: Brother Hollie
runners.spotlight: Never in contention when a remote seventh in Down Royal event won by Kalsman; not an obvious candidate.
runners.comment: Never in contention when a remote seventh in Down Royal event won by Kalsman

runners.name: Justicialism
runners.spotlight: Over four years since he won back-to-back handicap hurdles for Brian Hamilton; lightly raced in recent seasons; bounced back to form on second start for Noel Kelly when second to Kalsman over 2m4f at Down Royal last month; notched another second behind the improving Bella Union over 2m1f at Down Royal on latest; may go one better now.
runners.comment: Backed up second to Kalsman at Down Royal with another second at the same venue, respected

runners.name: Rajjnatin
runners.spotlight: Modest form in maidens and handicaps for his previous trainer; more encouraging on yard debut in 2m5f Down Royal handicap in March when well backed and finishing third; flopped when favourite at Wexford; cheekpieces now as well a usual tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Encouraging third at Down Royal on stable debut, flopped when favourite at Wexford

runners.name: Tuff Days
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-32 under rules; best of three point runs last season when runner-up at Dromahane last month; made a bad mistake at the ninth when a remote sixth over fences at Limerick ten days ago.
runners.comment: Record of 0-32 under rules; runner-up in a point last month; others much preferred

runners.name: Sekifi
runners.spotlight: Has never reached the first three; looked as if he might not be a lost cause judged on Bellewstown sixth; however, ran poorly at Limerick on Thursday
runners.comment: Fair sixth at Bellewstown, ran poorly at Limerick on Thursday, makes a quick reappearance

runners.name: Annie's Belle
runners.spotlight: Needs to eliminate jumping errors; fourth behind a stablemate of Justicialism on latest; needs to find a bit extra.
runners.comment: Needs to eliminate jumping errors, fourth behind a stablemate of Justicialism on latest

runners.name: Anodandawink
runners.spotlight: Out of his depth in maidens, always at big odds; finished second in a point-to-point when with Derek O'Connor; inauspicious start to handicaps at Clonmel; better when third over a longer trip here last time; could go close if jumping more fluently
runners.comment: Third over a longer trip here last time, could go close if jumping more fluently

runners.name: Inches Away
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing in four maiden hurdle outings early last year and has changed stable; can only be watched in the circumstances.
runners.comment: Showed nothing in four maiden hurdle outings early last year and has changed stable

runners.name: Glaoinn An Airgead
runners.spotlight: 0-14 over hurdles, never closer than fifth in a race of any description; up against it after a break.
runners.comment: 0-14 over hurdles, never closer than fifth in a race of any description, up against it

runners.name: Westin Pimpernel
runners.spotlight: Very weak form; only 3lb out of the handicap but impossible to make a case for her.
runners.comment: Very weak form, only 3lb out of the handicap but impossible to make a case for her

races.race_name: Midland Veterinary Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Le Diablo
runners.spotlight: Mild promise in four starts for Pat Doyle; 33-1 when pulled up on handicap debut last October; a revelation at Clonmel last month, making all for a 33-1 win; failed to replicate form off revised mark at the same venue on latest
runners.comment: Clonmel win came out of the blue, failed to replicate form at the same venue on latest

runners.name: Laurie Blue
runners.spotlight: Won a weak maiden hurdle at Roscommon last August; down the field in a handicap on next start and then ran twice over fences; fourth back over hurdles at Punchestown on recent return and has a place chance on that evidence.
runners.comment: Not the most consistent mare, place chance on the form of recent Punchestown fourth

runners.name: Benzine
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form over hurdles; returned from a break with an excellent first run over fences at Limerick, finished second despite making a few slight errors; strong chance if that improvement is maintained on his return to hurdling.
runners.comment: Returned from a break with an excellent first run over fences at Limerick, strong chance

runners.name: Premier Rogue
runners.spotlight: Placed in a bumper and maiden hurdles, down the field over C&D on his most recent start.
runners.comment: Placed in a bumper and maiden hurdles, down the field over C&D on his most recent start

runners.name: High Notions
runners.spotlight: Fairyhouse maiden hurdle winner last October (2m4f, yielding); struggled in a Down Royal Grade 3 next time; weak form in handicap hurdles so far; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Won a 2m4f maiden last October, weak form in handicap hurdles so far, hard to fancy
runners.owner: I'm Going To No Home Syndicate

runners.name: Chesterbaylad
runners.spotlight: Attracted support down to 15-2 at Down Royal, let down badly by his jumping; much more like it when second in a 19-runner contest at Punchestown last time; has an edge over Laurie Blue on that running and holds a leading chance.
runners.comment: Flopped when well backed at Down Royal, much more like it at Punchestown, leading chance

runners.name: Mount Hotham
runners.spotlight: Poor form in maidens; 50-1 when in rear at Tramore in his first handicap.
runners.comment: Poor form in maidens, 50-1 when in rear at Tramore in his first handicap

runners.name: Secret Rock
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle in 2023; also two Flat wins in 2024 for Tony Martin; lacks a recent run but of some interest on his first outing for Cian Collins.
runners.comment: Won a maiden hurdle in 2023, also two Flat wins in 204 for Tony Martin, of some interest

runners.name: Diamond Secret
runners.spotlight: Yet to reach the first four, relatively unexposed; may do better here on handicap debut
runners.comment: Yet to reach the first four, relatively unexposed, may do better here on handicap debut

runners.name: Walk Win
runners.spotlight: Hints of promise in maiden hurdle outings at Cork and Bellewstown; prominent for a long way at Punchestown in his first handicap; cheekpieces fitted now.
runners.comment: Prominent for a long way at Punchestown in his first handicap, cheekpieces fitted now

runners.name: Make The Plan
runners.spotlight: Off the course for a long time, chasing when last seen; best hurdles run was in 2023; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Off the course for a long time, chasing when last seen, best hurdles run was in 2023

runners.name: El Regalo
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two recent Flat outings; hurdles form is unconvincing; cheekpieces now.
runners.comment: Down the field in two recent Flat outings, hurdles form is unconvincing; cheekpieces now

runners.name: I Feel Fine
runners.spotlight: Still in contention when unseating two out at Limerick in November; disappointing favourite at the same venue at Christmas; again quite well fancied when pulled up at Down Royal in January.
runners.comment: Did not enjoy much fortune during the winter, beaten favourite on penultimate start

races.race_name: Geoff Scally Centra Summerhill Beginners Chase

runners.name: Billie Frechette
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose Flat winner; 1-6 over hurdles and rated 120; promise in first two chase attempts, earning a rating of 117; beaten 17l into second of 12 behind talented Supersundae here (22.5f, gd-yld) in April; 9l fourth of 10 at Killarney (2m5f, gd-yld) 22 days ago; leading claims.
runners.comment: Promising start to chase career, rated 120 over hurdles, leading claims in this company

runners.name: Chichester Park
runners.spotlight: Point winner is 1-6 over hurdles and rated 115; not a bad effort over 2m in a handicap hurdle at Punchestown latest and shaped like further would suit; dam related to a couple of useful chasers and yard coming into form; could go well.
runners.comment: Point winner, 1-6 over hurdles and rated 115, dam related to useful chasers, could go well

runners.name: Boston Rajj
runners.spotlight: Exposed hurdler 1-14 in that sphere and rated 113; his win came over 3m2f but he's effective at this shorter trip; however, not an obvious candidate for chasing on breeding and lacks a recent run; others appeal more.
runners.comment: 1-14 over hurdles and rated 113, not an obvious candidate for chasing on breeding

runners.name: Something Noble
runners.spotlight: Locally trained 4yo; Killarney maiden hurdle winner last July (2m1f, yld-sft); added a Limerick handicap hurdle off 112 in March (2m, soft); tailed off on latest at Bellewstown and now switches attention to chasing; would probably prefer softer ground.
runners.comment: 2-4 over hurdles and rated 118, tailed off over timber latest, would prefer softer ground

runners.name: Howdoyalikeherhome
runners.spotlight: Placed in a couple of points; 1-16 over hurdles and rated 119 in that sphere; however, was pulled up in successive races at Galway and Killarney when last seen last August and while there's a good chance he'll make a chaser on breeding, he's tended to need his comeback runs in the past.
runners.comment: 1-16 over hurdles and rated 119, pulled up when last seen in August, might need this run

runners.name: Jane Eire
runners.spotlight: 1-8 over hurdles and rated 118; modest efforts in first two chases at Fairyhouse when beaten 17l and Wexford when beaten 40l, both last October; off since and this is an easier race; yard continues in hot form so she can't be discounted.
runners.comment: 1-8 over hurdles, rated 118, modest first two chase runs in Oct' but can't discount

runners.name: Londonofficecallin
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose sort; a winner of six races on the Flat, including the Cork Derby on latest (1m4f, soft) on first run back after a break; 4-28 over hurdles and rated 120; makes a belated chase debut at the age of nine but not bred for this discipline; in good form right now though and can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Won Cork Derby on latest, rated 120 over hurdles, not bred for chasing but can't discount

runners.name: Apple's Of Bresil
runners.spotlight: Won a Gowran maiden hurdle and Fairyhouse novice in 2024; weakened over 22.5f on handicap hurdle debut at Ballinrobe on sole start in 2025; long layoff to overcome on chase debut but worth a market check.
runners.comment: Maiden and novice hurdle winner in 2024, only seen once in 2025, long layoff to overcome

runners.name: Jackson Lamb
runners.spotlight: Won back to back races last summer, a Bellewstown maiden hurdle (20.5f, good) and a Kelso novice (2m5f, good); out of depth in Cheltenham Grade 2 novice hurdle when last seen in October; not bred for this game and probably best watched after a 219-day absence.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner rated 119, not bred for this game, off 219 days, likley best watched

runners.name: Mister Frostie
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; 0-6 over hurdles and rated 103; pulled up and beaten 72l in Downpatrick maiden hunter chases the last twice; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 103 rated maiden hurdler, PU and soundly beaten in m'den hunter chases last twice

races.race_name: GUY Clothing & KODE Clothing & Footwear Handicap Chase

runners.name: Iamagetaway
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner over 2m4f on good; 0-7 as a chaser but has placed form over C&D; beaten favourite when a fair third here last month on stable debut; ran poorly when again the market leader at Killarney; may have lost confidence after a mistake at the first.
runners.comment: Third when favourite here on stable debut, needs to bounce back from a poor Killarney run

runners.name: The Dara Man
runners.spotlight: Modest strike-rate of 3-39 over fences with last win coming over C&D in August 2024; jumped badly when pulled up on final two starts in 2025; possibly needed the run at Limerick ten days ago.
runners.comment: Has the merit of being a C&D winner, modest strike rate, possibly needed a recent outing

runners.name: Gaelic Arc
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner, twice over fences; enjoyed a productive summer last year, scoring over C&D in June before finishing a nose second in a valuable event in Killarney; highly tried later in the year; made mistakes and finished in rear over C&D on return.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a productive summer last year, scored over C&D in June, in rear here on return
runners.owner: Mark R D Fleming / T Westropp Bennett

runners.name: Bridie's Beau
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner up to 2m4f; best chase form last winter was on heavy ground; best hurdles form also on soft/heavy; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Best chase form last winter was on heavy ground, best hurdles form also on soft/heavy

runners.name: Trasna Na Pairce
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back hurdling wins at Clonmel last summer; also front-running second behind Bridie's Beau at same venue in a 2m3f novice handicap chase; 0-6 over fences.
runners.comment: Won twice over hurdles early last season, 0-6 over fences, best run behind Bridie's Beau

runners.name: General Clermont
runners.spotlight: Two chase wins; went close at Listowel in September (2m6f, good to yielding); twice placed from three visits to this track; shaped quite well for this when fourth at Limerick ten days ago on first run after a break; made a lot of the running then, every chance with fitness advanced.
runners.comment: Shaped quite well for this when fourth at Limerick ten days ago on first run after a break

runners.name: Warriors Wood
runners.spotlight: Relished good ground last summer when winning over hurdles at this course and Ballinrobe and ran well in handicap hurdle at Downpatrick off 110; unseated when in contention three out at Tramore on chase debut; clear of the third when no match for a warm favourite over C&D 16 days ago; could be hard to beat now.
runners.comment: Clear of the third when no match for a warm favourite over C&D 16 days ago, sound chance

runners.name: Luimneach
runners.spotlight: Twice successful over hurdles; ordinary handicap chase form until second at Down Royal in January; pulled up at Thurles on his final start for the now-retired Des McDonogh; 10lb below hurdles mark; fair chance if replicating his improved display.
runners.comment: Now 10lb below hurdles mark, fair chance om the form of his penultimate start, yard debut

runners.name: Andys Flame
runners.spotlight: Ran only twice in 2025, won at Kelso on the first occasion, poor at Cartmel on the second; suited by good ground; may need the run.
runners.comment: Ran only twice in 2025, won at Kelso on the first occasion, poor at Cartmel on the second

runners.name: Vina Ardanza
runners.spotlight: Useful sort for Gordon Elliott early in his career, finished a remote third behind Jonbon and El Fabiolo in a Grade 1 contest at Aintree in 2023; mostly campaigned in points for the last two years, below his best in four outings in that sphere since January.
runners.comment: Useful sort for Gordon Elliott early in his career, ordinary form in points this year

runners.name: No Flies On Him
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner has been placed only twice in ten chase starts; best effort for a long time when going close at Limerick last November; lacks a recent run; first time with cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Best effort for a long time when going close at Limerick last November, no recent run

runners.name: Charlie Luciano
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdles winner achieved a peak rating of 129 in that sphere; 0-8 over fences and rated 114; pulled up at Fairyhouse on final 2025 start, and badly out of form over hurdles before that.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler, 0-8 over fences and rated 114; pulled up at Fairyhouse on final 2025 start

races.race_name: Follow Kilbeggan On Instagram (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Pack Ball
runners.spotlight: Made a promising start to his career at Killarney when keeping on for a 15l fifth of 15 (2m1f, gd-yld) at odds of 5-1; Patrick Mullins takes over now (5-14 for yard in bumpers) and with improvement likely to be forthcoming, he's the one to beat.
runners.comment: 5-1 and showed promise on Killarney debut, Patrick Mullins takes over, the one to beat

runners.name: Ballyteigue Bay
runners.spotlight: Remote third on debut in a Clonmel bumper a year ago; beaten 68l and pulled up in two maiden hurdles since; off since January; a repeat of that debut run would give him a place chance in this weak race.
runners.comment: Remote third on debut in Clonmel bumper, place chance if he can repeat that form

runners.name: Gowayoutathat
runners.spotlight: 4yo Getaway gelding; third foal; unraced dam out of unraced half-sister to smart 2m4f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Benvolio; yard 0-22 in bumpers last five seasons; probably best watched unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: 4yo Getaway gelding, dam unraced, yard 0-22 in bumpers last 5 seasons, watch

runners.name: Rarely Miss Taken
runners.spotlight: Poet's Word mare; half-sister to winners Bobby's Nelson (useful 2m4f hurdle) and Kalanisi Dubh (2m1f hurdle); dam point winner, sister to 2m3f-3m hurdle/chase winner Prince Massini; market check advised on debut.
runners.comment: Poet's Word mare, dam point winner, market check advised on debut

runners.name: Corway
runners.spotlight: Vadamos gelding; dam unplaced 1m-1m3f (RPR 60), sister to French 1m2f winner Melbourne Shuffle, half-sister to Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner Pichola Dance; yard 0-8 in bumpers last five seasons and tongue tied but still worth a market check.
runners.comment: Vadamos gelding, dam unplaced on Flat, tongue tied, check the market

runners.name: Ivebeenkewadesert
runners.spotlight: 4yo Kew Gardens gelding has suffered heavy defeats in maiden hurdles at Limerick (beaten 68l) and Cork on latest (beaten 140l); tongue tie now applied; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Kew Gardens gelding, beaten cumulative 208l in two maiden hurdles, tongue tie on

runners.name: Madison Twist
runners.spotlight: 4yo Headman gelding; dam unraced sister to useful French 2m1f-2m5f chase winner Jazz Manouche, out of maiden half-sister to French 2m6f Grade 2 chase winner Kario De Sormain; yard has gone a long time without a winner but makes some appeal on paper so check the market.
runners.comment: Headman gelding, dam sister to useful French chaser, some appeal on paper, check market

runners.name: Getaway Tobin
runners.spotlight: Getaway half-sister to the top class Monkfish but showed little when beaten 40l at odds of 28-1 on debut at Punchestown earlier this month; surely capable of better at some stage but may need more time.
runners.comment: Showed little on debut at Punchestown, well-related but may need more time

runners.name: O'Callaghan Can
runners.spotlight: 18-1 and pulled up in a Thurles bumper (2m, soft) on debut in February; rider booking a plus and could improve for better ground but can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Pulled up when 18-1 on debut in Thurles bumper, rider booking a plus but best watched

runners.name: Rokanako
runners.spotlight: 25-1 and weakened quickly in the last 2f when beaten 25l in a Tipperary bumper last July (2m, gd-yld); off since and can only be watched on return from a 332-day absence.
runners.comment: 25-1 and weakened quickly in the last 2f when beaten 25l in a Tipperary bumper last July

races.race_name: fakenhamdogfield.co.uk Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Madajovy
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice for Dan Skelton in the winter (2m3f/3m1f) but left that stable after a disappointing run in March and was pulled up after performing poorly for new yard three weeks ago (2m3f); back up in trip with cheekpieces refitted.
runners.comment: Left Dan Skelton after a disappointing effort and was pulled up on stable debut

runners.name: Tuba
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles, having been pulled up in both handicaps last year (2m1f/2m3f), but returns to this code after two 1m6f AW wins this spring and is evidenty in good nick; tries a new trip; likely contender.
runners.comment: Pulled up in both handicap hurdles last year but a dual AW winner this spring

runners.name: Popa Rich
runners.spotlight: Pulled up at Perth this month, after a layoff, but was placed twice on the tight left-handed track at Cartmel (2m6f) last summer, so there's a good chance Fakenham will also suit him; big player if back on song.
runners.comment: Pulled up this month, after a layoff, but has good claims if judged on last summer's form

runners.name: The Kings Soldier
runners.spotlight: Made fairly encouraging handicap debut when keeping-on fourth at Stratford (3m2f, good) last month but was soundly beaten at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good) four weeks ago and it's hard to know what to expect from him here; 2lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Ran okay on last month's handicap debut (3m2f) but soundly beaten since (2m7f)

runners.name: Dame In Disguise
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing more than glimmers of promise in her three qualifying races (2m-2m4f), albeit she had an excuse when pulled up last time (stumbled and lost action); makes handicap debut in a weak race, though, and the new trip may enable improvement.
runners.comment: Yet to show anything more than glimmers of promise but makes handicap debut in a weak race

races.race_name: Bruce Towers & Sons Mechanical Engineers Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Dancing Dame
runners.spotlight: 0-8 in bumpers and 0-4 over hurdles when based in Ireland but belied 200-1 odds when very respectable sixth of 19 in good-quality Naas maiden (2m, soft to heavy) on latest outing in December; should be okay on the much quicker ground here and new stable tends to do well with new recruits.
runners.comment: Very respectable sixth in good-quality Naas maiden in December; now with new stable

runners.name: Skye Glance
runners.spotlight: Sister to connections' smart jumper Millers Bank; beaten under 6l and appeared to display improvement when fifth of seven in good-ground Plumpton bumper last month but the value of that form is open to question; makes hurdle debut here; others look much stronger.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in latest bumper but value of that form is open to question

runners.name: Alibey
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in three 2m handicaps this spring (good/good to firm), twice at Plumpton and once at Huntingdon; that form isn't anything to go wild about and her tendency to race too freely remains a nagging concern but she has another big role to play here.
runners.comment: Runner-up in three 2m handicaps this spring and has another big role to play here

runners.name: Baileys Big Image
runners.spotlight: Tailed off when 100-1 for good-ground Fontwell bumper on rules debut in September; not seen since.
runners.comment: Tailed off when 100-1 for good-ground Fontwell bumper on rules debut in September

runners.name: Never Told Me
runners.spotlight: Displayed clear promise in three good-ground maiden hurdles last summer, perhaps most notably when second of five at Newton Abbot (2m1f) on latest appearance; had wind op in September and should have more to offer this year; hood removed after one use.
runners.comment: Displayed clear promise in three good-ground maiden hurdles last summer; back from a break

races.race_name: Book Your Function @fakenhamracecourse.co.uk Handicap Chase

runners.name: Getaway Jewel
runners.spotlight: Four chase wins, the latest off today's mark in this race a year ago (good ground); absent since some below-par efforts on slower-than-ideal ground towards end of 2025 and not getting any younger but booking of Sean Bowen drops strong hint he'll be primed for a bold defence.
runners.comment: Off since some below-par runs in late 2025 but won this last year and Sean Bowen rides

runners.name: Most Wanted
runners.spotlight: Won a low-grade Flat handicap last summer but has modest record over hurdles and was well beaten at Taunton (2m, good) last month, after a layoff; might be aided by refitted cheekpieces on this chasing debut but is not bred for fences; risky.
runners.comment: Won Class 6 Flat handicap in 2025 but has modest hurdle record and is not bred for chasing

runners.name: Not For Passin
runners.spotlight: Second in a point before readily made all in good-ground Perth bumper for an Irish stable last spring; made underwhelming start to hurdling career for new stable last season, with best of three runs his well-held second at Catterick (1m7f, good) in January, but switches to fences for this handicap debut and has plenty of scope for improvement.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; low-key over hurdles but switches to fences for handicap debut; can improve

runners.name: Chateau Du Lys
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over fences; seemed to respond well to first-time cheekpieces when second at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) last month but was only third best on merit there and was tailed off on his latest outing; 1lb wrong here.
runners.comment: Only third best on merit when second at Market Rasen last month; tailed off since

races.race_name: 1st The Queens' Dragoon Guards Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Blackwater Lilly
runners.spotlight: Twice made the frame at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) for Brian Ellison last month; left that yard after a disappointing Flat run but was in contention before awkward jumps at last two flights proved costly on recent stable debut at Newton Abbot (2m2f, good); swaps usual cheekpices for first-time visor; can make her presence felt.
runners.comment: Ran well on recent stable debut; can make presence felt again in new headgear

runners.name: An Bradan Feasa
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles (2m/2m4f, good) and twice on the Flat in 2025; looked as good as ever when second on the Flat this month, after a break, and is respected, albeit the nagging concern is that he ran poorly on his only previous visit to Fakenham.
runners.comment: Looked as good as ever on the Flat this month, after a break; respected back over hurdles

runners.name: Ambiente Friendly
runners.spotlight: Smart 3yo on the Flat; just 1-5 over hurdles, and his habit of racing far too freely remains a worry, but he wasn't beaten far in a valuable novices' handicap at Sandown (2m, good) last month and is in much calmer waters here.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in valuable novices' handicap last month; this race is much easier

runners.name: Hello Sweety
runners.spotlight: Free-going front-runner who won twice over C&D last season (soft/good to soft), both times by wide margins in modest mares' races; probably went off too hard when well beaten at Huntingdon last month but steadied the ship with respectable Flat run 24 days ago; sure to be suited by return to this track but stiil needs to prove she can cope with a mark this high.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner last season, both times by wide margins; ran okay on the Flat recently

races.race_name: Prince Of Wales Cup Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: No But I Will
runners.spotlight: Just held on to make winning stable debut over C&D (soft) in December but failed to build upon an encouraging reappearance run when tailed off at Market Rasen last month; others have more persuasive profiles.
runners.comment: Won over C&D on stable debut in December but tailed off last time; others appeal more
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Donald Smith

runners.name: Nickelforce
runners.spotlight: 3-14 over fences, the latest win gained at Wincanton (2m4f, good) in November; just a respectable third at Uttoxeter (2m5f, good) this month, his second start after a layoff, and remains on fairly tough mark but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Three chase wins; just a respectable third at Uttoxeter this month but still considered

runners.name: Tommy Cullen
runners.spotlight: Hit prolonged flat spots before keeping on for modest fourth over 2m on latest two appearances, the latest at Warwick this month; on a good mark now and this step back up in trip could be the right move but it remains to be seen whether Jack Quinlan can keep him in touch this time.
runners.comment: Hit flat spots before keeping on over 2m on last two outings; back up in trip here

runners.name: Hurricane Bay
runners.spotlight: Well below best last season but exploited some help from the handicapper when winning in pretty good style at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) this month; even after a 5lb rise, he's still 5lb lower than when winning this race in 2025; good chance.
runners.comment: Won off reduced mark this month; still 5lb lower than bagging this prize in 2025

runners.name: Coastguard Station
runners.spotlight: Good-ground specialist who was a course winner in 2024 and returns here after two good efforts this spring, the latter a close fourth at Aintree (2m4f, good) 16 days ago; respected.
runners.comment: Course winner in 2024 and returns here after two decent efforts; respected

races.race_name: Fisher Electrical Contractors Female Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Female Pro Jockeys/Amateur Riders')

runners.name: Gone In Sixty
runners.spotlight: Safely held when fourth over fences at Fontwell (2m2f, good) this month but that was his first run since November (also his first after a wind op); reverts to hurdling on mark just 2lb higher then when winning this race by 14l on good ground a year ago; must be considered.
runners.comment: Safely held over fences this month, after a break, but won this race by 14l in 2025

runners.name: Winston's Oath
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who displayed significant promise when third of six against own age-group over C&D (good) last month; well beaten on both hurdling starts either side of that but his course form brings him into the equation on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo; by far the best of his three hurdle runs was over C&D; handicap debut

runners.name: Market House
runners.spotlight: Consistent on the Flat/over hurdles this year, and deserved last month's narrow win at Plumpton (2m, good; only time he wore a visor); can remain very competitive after 3lb rise.
runners.comment: Consistent 6yo who deserved last month's narrow win at Plumpton; likely contender again

runners.name: Nottodaybobo
runners.spotlight: Has turned a corner since returning from a break this spring and came good for current stable with front-running win at Market Rasen (2m4f, good) three weeks ago; that form looks modest but he ought to give another good account here.
runners.comment: Has turned a corner since returning from a break and made all at Market Rasen this month

runners.name: Secret Ingredient
runners.spotlight: Front-running second of six on 5f debut here and was slowly away when trailing in last in Spice Bag's race three weeks ago; can do better.
runners.comment: Slowly away when trailing in last in Spice Bag's race three weeks ago; can do better

runners.name: Talents Champion
runners.spotlight: Just behind Secret Ingredient on debut but he folded tamely when behind a handful of these last time out.
runners.comment: He folded tamely when behind a handful of these last time out

runners.name: Silvery Knight
runners.spotlight: Behind two of these on his debut and behind several of today's rivals on his next start; clearly has to step up.
runners.comment: Behind two of these on his debut and behind several of today's rivals on his next start

runners.name: Sharp Planet
runners.spotlight: Trial winner who was sent off favourite under Moreira for his 5f debut here and was only overhauled late on by Spice Bag; that was a promising start but today's draw is troublesome.
runners.comment: Second to Spice Bag over 5f here on debut; today's draw is troublesome
runners.owner: Yiu King Chuen, Chino Yiu Chin Hang & Chris Yiu Chun Hang

runners.name: Chancheng Sparkle
runners.spotlight: Makes official debut after form figures of 67734 in trials; probably best to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Makes official debut after form figures of 67734 in trials; likely best to look elsewhere

runners.name: Coogee Bay
runners.spotlight: Well down the field in four trial races this year and he'd be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Well down the field in four trial races this year and he'd be a surprise winner
runners.owner: Alex Leung King Yue & Mok Tsz Wan

runners.name: Golden Gunners
runners.spotlight: Looks opposable on what he's shown in trials and especially drawn in 11.
runners.comment: Looks opposable on what he's shown in trials and especially drawn in 11

runners.name: Quantum Wukong
runners.spotlight: Third in a trial but comfortably beaten in the other two and he's got a wicked draw.
runners.comment: Third in a trial but comfortably beaten in the other two and he's got a wicked draw

runners.name: Rising World
runners.spotlight: Won a trial in late April and has since finished second in another under Zac Purton, who retains the ride for this official debut; that alone heightens interest.
runners.comment: Won a trial in late April and has since finished second in another under Zac Purton

runners.name: Almighty Warrior
runners.spotlight: Looked nothing out of the ordinary in his trials and was behind four of today's rivals over 5f here 22 days ago; Zac Purton rode that day but not now.
runners.comment: Behind four of today's rivals over 5f here 22 days ago; Zac Purton gets off

runners.name: Show Me Your Love
runners.spotlight: Has won a trial but also fluffed the start in another and he didn't break well when behind a few of these here 22 days ago; this longer trip may help but he's out in 12.
runners.comment: He didn't break well when behind a few of these here 22 days ago

runners.name: Ever Wealth
runners.spotlight: Trial winner in March and runner-up in another since finishing third behind Spice Bag and Sharp Planet here 22 days ago when he showed bright speed to lead early before fading; well drawn to attack from stall 3 and Nichola Yuen rides again.
runners.comment: A front-running third behind two of these here recently; well drawn to attack

runners.name: Gaudium Magnum
runners.spotlight: Drawn on the wing when fourth to Spice Bag here recently so that was an encouraging start, and he was well found in the market; can go well from a kinder box this time.
runners.comment: Drawn on the wing when fourth to Spice Bag here recently so that was an encouraging start

runners.name: Spice Bag
runners.spotlight: Failed to make a strong impression in trials but he came from off the pace to spring a surprise on his debut here over 5f, beating Sharp Planet and others in this race; carries a penalty but did well to win from where he was three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Carries a penalty but did well to win from where he was three weeks ago
runners.owner: Lai Wai Chi & Adrian Michael Bell

runners.name: Looking Bright
runners.spotlight: Close up over C&D on consecutive outings in February/March, but his form has dipped; others preferred.
runners.comment: Close up over C&D on consecutive outings in February/March, but his form has dipped

runners.name: Comet Radiance
runners.spotlight: Reserve; hasn't kicked on at all since finishing a close second over C&D in March.
runners.comment: Reserve; hasn't kicked on at all since finishing a close second over C&D in March

runners.name: Quick Money
runners.spotlight: Only win in 15 came in a 5f novice and it's been hard work in handicaps despite a falling mark.
runners.comment: Only win in 15 was in a novice and it's been hard work in handicaps despite a falling mark

runners.name: Jumbo Blessing
runners.spotlight: Disappointing favourite under Moreira at Happy Valley last time but that was over 6f and two of his three previous runs (all at 5f) were highly encouraging; not yet exposed.
runners.comment: Disappointing last time but two of his three previous runs were highly encouraging
runners.owner: Billy Chan Wing Kwong, Kiki Chan Yan Ki & A Chan Fong Ki

runners.name: Grand Nova
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; not sure what happened when well behind in the race won by Oneshot but he's been very competitive on either side of that at Happy Valley; not without hope from a useful draw.
runners.comment: Poor run behind Oneshot here but very competitive on either side of that; well drawn

runners.name: Casa Primo
runners.spotlight: All his runs have been respectable and he ran into some traffic when fifth in Oneshot's race here last time; possible.
runners.comment: He ran into some traffic when fifth in Oneshot's race here last time; possible

runners.name: The All Out
runners.spotlight: Dual 5f novice winner who hasn't threatened in handicaps and has had a few chances now; unable to win any of his trials since last seen on the track in December.
runners.comment: Dual 5f novice winner who hasn't threatened in handicaps and has had a few chances now

runners.name: Oneshot
runners.spotlight: Tapped into his debut promise when ridden out for a near-length victory over C&D three weeks ago with some of these behind; raised 6lb but has an even better draw now.
runners.comment: Raised 6lb for recent C&D success but he has an even better draw now
runners.owner: Anthony Chan Wai Choi, Wong Tsz Wa & Kwok Ying Lung

runners.name: Giant Spirit
runners.spotlight: Did well in Australia but hasn't got a lot going in Hong Kong and remains opposable.
runners.comment: Did well in Australia but hasn't got a lot going in Hong Kong and remains opposable
runners.owner: Dr V Chan Chi Tao, John Tang Lam Chek & Uri Yau Wing Hay

runners.name: Storm Mirror
runners.spotlight: Winner of two trials and when seventh behind Oneshot here he got no luck in running when odds of 17-5; Purton takes over this time and he's one to be interested in.
runners.comment: When seventh behind Oneshot he got no luck in running; Purton takes over

runners.name: Winalot
runners.spotlight: Reserve; took a few goes to win a trial and was only midfield on C&D debut 22 days ago.
runners.comment: Reserve; took a few goes to win a trial and was only midfield on C&D debut 22 days ago

runners.name: Double Alpha
runners.spotlight: Made all in a 4f barrier trial this month and ideally drawn to try something similar on official debut.
runners.comment: Made all in a 4f barrier trial this month and ideally drawn to try something similar

runners.name: Gallant Bounty
runners.spotlight: The winner of two trials but standards have dropped in his final two, finishing eighth and sixth.
runners.comment: The winner of two trials but standards have dropped in his final two

runners.name: Ka Ying Lightning
runners.spotlight: Has accrued plenty of experience in trials, winning two from eight; could be a factor on debut.
runners.comment: Has accrued plenty of experience in trials, winning two from eight; could be a factor

runners.name: King's Arrow
runners.spotlight: Couple of thirds in this year's two runs in trials but could be vulnerable on official debut.
runners.comment: Couple of thirds in this year's two runs in trials but could be vulnerable on debut
runners.owner: Jameson Leung Tai Lin & Rene Leung Yan Ting

runners.name: Turf Quest
runners.spotlight: Mostly well held in his six trials, faring no better than fourth; others preferred, especially from stall 2.
runners.comment: Mostly well held in his six trials, faring no better than fourth; others preferred
runners.owner: Dr John Shum Ding Ping & Dr Lawrence Chan Pui Wing

runners.name: Flash Star
runners.spotlight: Surprise 40-1 winner over 6f at Happy Valley in December but has not built on that performance; drawn very wide.
runners.comment: Surprise 40-1 winner over 6f at Happy Valley in December but hasn't kicked on

runners.name: Tin Fook
runners.spotlight: Not well drawn last time but placed on dirt the run before and he's been running in Class 3s; possible.
runners.comment: Not well drawn last time but placed on dirt the run before and he's been in Class 3s

runners.name: Happy Brethren
runners.spotlight: Looked sure to win over C&D in March before overhauled late on; again only 2l away next time at Happy Valley (6f) and he had a bad draw over 7f here last time; unfortunately, he has another tricky one to overcome.
runners.comment: Can win races off this mark but unfortunately he has another tricky draw to overcome

runners.name: Bullish Pride
runners.spotlight: Reserve; all seven defeats here and hasn't been shaping like a winner waiting to happen.
runners.comment: Reserve; all seven defeats here and hasn't been shaping like a winner waiting to happen
runners.owner: Wong Wing Keung & Cheung Ka Ho

runners.name: One More
runners.spotlight: Had been contesting Class 3s until last time here over 7f when he was scuppered by a bad draw; that's no excuse this time and he's dropped to a good mark; considered.
runners.comment: Had been contesting Class 3s until last time over 7f when he was scuppered by a bad draw
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Johnny Ng Sai Chun Et Al

runners.name: Fun N Fun Together
runners.spotlight: Seemingly back in good form but both wins and best figures have come on dirt; could still have some part to play from stall 1 with Nichola Yuen back aboard.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in good form but both wins and best figures have come on dirt

runners.name: Ka Ying Radiance
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and he's shown enough to believe his turn will come, especially having captured a recent trial; however, yet again he's dropped unlucky on the draw front.
runners.comment: His turn will come, but yet again he's dropped unlucky on the draw front

runners.name: Papaya Brose
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D on debut and although a beaten favourite in two subsequent races, neither went to plan (getting behind after interference); he can win again off this mark.
runners.comment: C&D debut winner and not a lot has gone right for him in two races since

runners.name: Supreme Voyager
runners.spotlight: Clear claims on his head defeat here in March and recent trial win bodes well.
runners.comment: Clear claims on his head defeat here in March and recent trial win bodes well
runners.owner: 24/25 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: View All Things
runners.spotlight: Ready winner over 7f here this month but only ninth off this 8lb higher mark last week and this drop back in trip may not be ideal.
runners.comment: Ready winner over 7f here this month but went up 8lb and was only ninth last week

runners.name: Aca Fast
runners.spotlight: Underwhelming in trials before a heavy defeat over C&D 22 days ago (40-1).
runners.comment: Underwhelming in trials before a heavy defeat over C&D 22 days ago (40-1)

runners.name: Hyannis Star
runners.spotlight: Reserve; yet to get competitive in handicaps and opposable despite recent trial win.
runners.comment: Reserve; yet to get competitive in handicaps and opposable despite recent trial win

runners.name: Happy Smile
runners.spotlight: A mixed bag in trials but did well to finish sixth of 14 here four weeks ago after missing the break; could have improvement in him.
runners.comment: Did well to finish sixth of 14 here four weeks ago after missing the break
runners.owner: Chan Tung, Lo Wing Man Et Al

runners.name: Apex Glory
runners.spotlight: Has won a trial but offered little here a month ago when 50-1 for official debut.
runners.comment: Has won a trial but offered little here a month ago when 50-1 for official debut

runners.name: Speedy Power
runners.spotlight: Beaten in trials before finishing seventh from a wide draw over C&D a fortnight ago; perhaps one for another day.
runners.comment: Beaten in trials before finishing seventh from a wide draw over C&D a fortnight ago

runners.name: Smiling Champion
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two previous runs over C&D and since well held in a trial; a worthy outsider from stall 14.
runners.comment: Down the field in two previous runs over C&D and since well held in a trial
runners.owner: Ho Tze Man, Martin Lee Ka Shing & Joseph Tse Lap Bun

runners.name: A Time For Us
runners.spotlight: Right there over this trip at Happy Valley in March but standards have dropped since; however, he's clearly capable as that last positive run was off 6lb higher, and he's got a good draw.
runners.comment: On a good mark so difficult to rule out from a favourable draw

runners.name: Oldtown
runners.spotlight: Hasn't fired as he can in two runs for new stable but he's beneath his winning mark, well drawn and partnered by Zac Purton; possibilities.
runners.comment: He's beneath his winning mark, well drawn and partnered by Zac Purton

runners.name: Savvy Warrior
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual winner in Britain; off a reduced mark he was fourth over 1m at Happy Valley recently but that might be his trip.
runners.comment: Reserve; off a reduced mark he was fourth at Happy Valley recently, albeit over 1m

runners.name: Get Friendly
runners.spotlight: Reserve; disappointing since an encouraging debut here but he won a trial this month, so all hope is not yet lost.
runners.comment: Reserve; disappointing since encouraging debut here but he won a trial this month

runners.name: Master Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Last two defeats were over 7f and he's better at 6f, over which he was a close fourth here in March; 4lb lower than that day.
runners.comment: On his last run over 6f he was a close fourth here in March when 4lb higher

runners.name: Malpensa
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced improver who got off the mark on return to turf here four weeks ago; didn't have a lot in hand and he's up 6lb, so probably needs more again.
runners.comment: Recent course winner; didn't have much in hand and up 6lb, so probably needs more again
runners.owner: 24/25 Chris So Wai Yin Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Joy Star
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in five official races and has to come with risks despite winning a trial this month.
runners.comment: Well beaten in five races and has to come with risks despite winning a trial this month
runners.owner: Lam King Wai & Dr & Mrs Lam Kai Cheong

runners.name: Speedy Smartie
runners.spotlight: Has been a model of consistency at 5f/6f here and at Happy Valley and he's run well from dodgy draws before; couldn't rule him out.
runners.comment: Has been a model of consistency at 5f/6f here and at Happy Valley
runners.owner: Dr Andrew Yu, Dr Alvin Yu Man Ho & Dr Bernice Yu Man Yan

runners.name: The Concentration
runners.spotlight: Mark took a hit for doing well in Class 5s; only seventh in two Class 4s, though Nichola Yuen keeps the faith after last time and this belated return to 6f is worth exploring.
runners.comment: Mark took a hit for doing well in Class 5s; only seventh in two Class 4s; down in trip

runners.name: E Ho Ho
runners.spotlight: Won in Australia but it's been a quiet start in Hong Kong and he had a good draw last time as well.
runners.comment: Won in Australia but a quiet start in Hong Kong and he had a good draw last time as well

runners.name: Regrowth Winner
runners.spotlight: Beaten double-digit lengths in all his races and impossible to make a case.
runners.comment: Beaten double-digit lengths in all his races and impossible to make a case
runners.owner: Chan Wing Kwan, Chan Wai Leung & Chadwick Mok Cham Hung

runners.name: Emerging Star
runners.spotlight: No joy in trials and has finished stone-cold last in his four official races, the last two handicaps.
runners.comment: Has finished stone-cold last in his four official races, the last two handicaps

runners.name: Turbo Jefferies
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo but couldn't make a case on what he's shown so far.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 3yo but couldn't make a case on what he's shown so far
runners.owner: Simmy Leung, Jan Lai et al

runners.name: Synergy Express
runners.spotlight: Modest in his trials and has looked no better on the track; readily opposed.
runners.comment: Modest in his trials and has looked no better on the track; readily opposed

runners.name: Dancing Blaze
runners.spotlight: Has performed okay in his trials without managing to win one and a similar tale under Moreira on recent debut over C&D; he wasn't strong through the line then but he was in the market and could yet do better.
runners.comment: Wasn't strong through the line here recently but he was in the market; may do better

runners.name: View Of Cosmos
runners.spotlight: Won a recent trial but he's not beaten a rival in two races on the track, at 6f and 7f.
runners.comment: Won a recent trial but he's not beaten a rival in two races on the track, at 6f and 7f

runners.name: Windlord
runners.spotlight: Classy in Britain for Andrew Balding but hasn't progressed at all from an encouraging debut in Hong Kong and this trip is on the tight side for him.
runners.comment: Ultimately disappointing since arriving in Hong Kong and 7f is on the tight side for him

runners.name: Uranus Star
runners.spotlight: Close up over C&D in March but two lesser efforts have followed and he now has to kick on again, which won't be easy from stall 11.
runners.comment: Close up in March but two lesser efforts have followed; revival needed from stall 11

runners.name: Winning Gold
runners.spotlight: Better known as a miler and was badly out of form in early part of this year; beaten in recent trials.
runners.comment: More of a miler and badly out of form in early part of this year; beaten in recent trials

runners.name: Kansas
runners.spotlight: Useful sprinter in Ireland but has shown very little in four Hong Kong runs, from 5f to 7f.
runners.comment: Useful sprinter in Ireland but has shown very little in four Hong Kong runs, from 5f to 7f

runners.name: King Dance
runners.spotlight: From off the pace he's got up in the nick of time to win two Class 4s over C&D; this is tougher after a 7lb rise but he's progressing; unwise to read too much into latest trial defeat.
runners.comment: From off the pace he's got up in the nick of time to win two Class 4s over C&D

runners.name: Positive Smile
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been in Class 4s here over 7f, and it was at that level when third over 6f here 22 days ago; this loftier level seems to test him.
runners.comment: Both wins and his latest third came in Class 4s; this level seems to test him

runners.name: Target Audience
runners.spotlight: Made all over 6f on dirt here in April but combination of a 6lb rise and Class 3 company found him out next time; nonetheless, Jose Moreira is back aboard and he's got a good draw to work with, albeit with stamina queries over this far.
runners.comment: Bit to prove at this level but he's dropped lucky with the draw for a front-runner

runners.name: Aeroinvincible
runners.spotlight: Well drawn for a front-runner and it was no backward step over C&D last time for all that his hat-trick bid came up short; respected off the same mark.
runners.comment: Well drawn for a front-runner and it was no backward step over C&D last time

runners.name: Red Maestro
runners.spotlight: Impressive on only Irish win, after which he had a long absence; has returned in good order and beat all bar the favourite over C&D (soft) four weeks ago; second in a trial since; in the mix.
runners.comment: Returned in good order and beat all bar the favourite over C&D (soft) four weeks ago
runners.owner: The Hon Ronald Arculli GBM GBS JP & Johanna K J Arculli BBS

runners.name: Fortune Link
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; has a bad draw but that was also the case when edging home over C&D under Nichola Yuen in April; that was on good ground, whereas it was soft when only tenth four weeks ago; can be given another chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner on good and it was soft ground when disappointing four weeks ago
runners.owner: Mixion Lam Ting Kwok & Luo Hong Fen

runners.name: Helene Supafeeling
runners.spotlight: First two wins were at 1m so he did well to collect over 6f here during the winter and posted another fine effort in defeat over 7f here a month ago; solid claims.
runners.comment: In form and very capable from 6f to 1m; solid claims

runners.name: Sovereign Fund
runners.spotlight: Successful yard debut over Happy Valley's 6f in February but has an overall strike-rate of 3-33 and can struggle with consistency.
runners.comment: Has an overall strike-rate of 3-33 and can struggle with consistency
runners.owner: Glenn Chan Wai Cheung & Raymond Chan Ka Shun

runners.name: Savvy Brilliant
runners.spotlight: Both wins here but over 6f; he's dropped to a tempting mark and bad interference has cost him the last twice; all his form is at sprint trips but he could easily stay the 7f; tempting under Purton.
runners.comment: Luckless the last twice; all his form is sprinting but he could easily stay the 7f

runners.name: Circuit Champion
runners.spotlight: Some concerns that he's been beaten in trials this month but he was progressive back in the spring, going back-to-back in handicaps over 6f (the latest a Class 3); won that nicely to earn this 8lb higher mark; has to be respected.
runners.comment: Beaten in recent trials but he was progressive over 6f back in the spring
runners.owner: Cheung Kwok Wing & Cheung Kwok Keung

runners.name: Charity Together
runners.spotlight: Consistent before narrow C&D win over Volcanic Spark in March and may not have been suited by soft ground when behind that rival last time but others in this field look better handicapped.
runners.comment: Consistent before narrow C&D win in March; others in this line-up look better handicapped

runners.name: Star Brose
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 1m3f win and was placed on consecutive starts in March (1m3f/1m2f); needs to better last couple of efforts but booking of Zac Purton catches the eye.
runners.comment: Needs to better last couple of efforts but booking of Zac Purton catches the eye

runners.name: Aestheticism
runners.spotlight: Stays this trip but has regressed this season and needs a revival.
runners.comment: Stays this trip but has regressed this season and needs a revival

runners.name: Ocean Impact
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with pretty good fourth at Happy Valley (1m3f; no headgear) last month and is now back on same mark as when winning in first-time blinkers there (1m1f) in November; contender if boosted by refitted cheekpieces here.
runners.comment: Returned to form at Happy Valley last month and is now back on last winning mark

runners.name: Kyrus Treasure
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all eight starts; kept on for respectable fourth in 1m1f contest won by Romantic Fantasy a fortnight ago and looks well worth a crack at this longer trip but needs to take another step forward.
runners.comment: 0-8; kept on for respectable fourth here (1m1f) recently; now needs to build on that run

runners.name: Enthralled
runners.spotlight: Steadily regressive 5yo who is yet to win this season; suited by this trip and lurks on a dangerous mark but never really looked dangerous when ninth here (1m) a week ago.
runners.comment: Lurks on a dangerous mark but this steadily regressive 5yo is yet to win this season

runners.name: Red Brick Warrior
runners.spotlight: Reserve; yet to be placed after seven starts but his strong-finishing fourth over 1m at Happy Valley in March makes him of some interest over this new trip.
runners.comment: 0-7 but finished well when fourth over 1m in March; of some interest over new trip

runners.name: Joyful Prosperity
runners.spotlight: Dug deep to win Class 5 event at Happy Valley (1m3f) in March and again ran well when third behind Super Goldendragon in this higher grade over that C&D last month; well held last time (albeit on soft ground), though, and needs to kick on again to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Game Class 5 winner in March (1m3f); needs to kick on again in this higher grade
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Silas S S Yang & Michelle H Y Yang

runners.name: Romantic Fantasy
runners.spotlight: Led close home to pip Super Goldendragon over 1m1f here a fortnight ago and will be suited by this extra furlong but a 5lb rise is probably enough to prevent a follow up from this exposed 6yo.
runners.comment: Narrow winner here this month; 5lb rise is probably enough to thwart this exposed 6yo

runners.name: Circuit Marshal
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; suggested his turn might be near when second over 1m here in March but two lesser efforts have followed (cheekpieces added last time); moves up in trip with a point to prove.
runners.comment: 0-6; runner-up over 1m in March but two lesser efforts followed; upped in trip today

runners.name: Massive Glory
runners.spotlight: Made quite promising debut over 7f here last month but was well beaten over 1m a fortnight later; not easy to predict over this even longer trip.
runners.comment: Made promising debut over 7f here last month but was well beaten over 1m a fortnight later

runners.name: Super Goldendragon
runners.spotlight: Displayed significant improvement to cause 88-1 shock when upped to 1m3f at Happy Valley last month, and backed up that performance with close second behind Romantic Fantasy here (1m1f) a fortnight ago; not yet fully exposed; features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Backed up last month's shock 1m3f win with close second over 1m1f two weeks ago
runners.owner: Yip Shu Bun & Jonathan Chok Shee Feng

runners.name: Shamz
runners.spotlight: Last of 14 over 1m here last month and has now been unplaced all eight Hong Kong starts; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Unplaced all eight Hong Kong starts and difficult to enthuse over

runners.name: Double Win
runners.spotlight: Won twice (1m2f/1m3f) in early part of this season and has been placed over 1m3f on two of his last three appearances; still on a workable mark and can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Placed over 1m3f on two of last three appearances and remains on a workable mark
runners.owner: Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk & Ma Yuen Yu

runners.name: Volcanic Spark
runners.spotlight: Close second to Charity Together over C&D two starts ago and coped well with the soft ground when leading close home over 1m1f here last time; might be vulnerable after latest 5lb rise but should run well again.
runners.comment: Might be vulnerable after recent 5lb rise but is in good form and this C&D suits

runners.name: Serangoon
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won quite stylishly over 1m1f here in February but has not been at the same level since and is still 6lb above that mark.
runners.comment: Won quite stylishly over 1m1f here in February but has not been at the same level since

runners.name: Beauty Joy
runners.spotlight: Four-time Group winner, most recently over 1m1f here last June, but well below best since pleasing seasonal debut in October and not getting any younger.
runners.comment: Well below best since pleasing reappearance in October and not getting any younger
runners.owner: Eleanor Kwok Law Kwai Chun & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen

runners.name: Stunning Peach
runners.spotlight: Took form to new level when winning twice in February/March (1m/1m1f) but recent efforts suggest the handicapper has regained control.
runners.comment: Won twice in February/March but recent efforts suggest handicapper has regained control

runners.name: Light Years Charm
runners.spotlight: Won five 7f handicaps here in 2025 but has not been in quite the same form this year, albeit he kept on for a respectable fourth two starts ago; should be okay back at 1m but needs help from the first-time tongue-tie and/or cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Won five 7f handicaps in 2025 but not quite in the same form this year; new headgear

runners.name: Sunlight Power
runners.spotlight: Excelled himself when close second in C&D Group 2 in November and has held his own at Group 1 level since but his overall record is rather mixed and others have more persuasive profiles.
runners.comment: Second in C&D Group 2 in November but overall record is rather mixed

runners.name: Patch Of Cosmo
runners.spotlight: Placed over 1m1f here in Classic Cup in March before keeping on for commendable fourth in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) later that month; flopped when favourite for 1m1f handicap last month and might not be ideally suited by this drop back in trip by may yet resume his progress.
runners.comment: Disappointed last time but was commendable fourth in Hong Derby two runs ago

runners.name: Chancheng Glory
runners.spotlight: Placed in 1m2f Group 1 last season but not really firing in all cylinders this term and might be vulnerable to some of the up-and-comers.
runners.comment: Not really firing on all cylinders this season; might be vulnerable to up-and-comers

runners.name: Sky Jewellery
runners.spotlight: Scored in good style over C&D four weeks ago and now has 5-7 strike-rate; probably still on the upgrade and needs to be taken very seriously on first attempt at a Group race.
runners.comment: Has 5-7 strike-rate after this month's stylish C&D win; respected in first Group race

runners.name: Winning Ovation
runners.spotlight: Rapidly progressive 4yo who readily won a 7f handicap here last month and is now 3-4 over that trip this year; will be a danger to all if suited by this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Rapidly progressive over 7f this year (three wins); big player if this new trip suits

runners.name: Copartner Prance
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; safely held in Group 1 races on last three appearances but has much better chance at this lesser level and is now 5lb lower than when second in C&D Group 2 handicap in October.
runners.comment: Has found things tough in Group 1s this season but has much better chance at this level

races.race_name: Federation Of Qing Yuan Associations (Hong Kong) Cup (Class 4) (Course B) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Never Peter Out
runners.spotlight: Gained all four wins over 1m on dirt; below form over that C&D on latest outing and opposable on this rare turf start.
runners.comment: Gained all four wins over 1m on dirt; opposable on this rare turf start

runners.name: Prestige Always
runners.spotlight: Without a win since September 2024 but ran respectably from wide stalls in 6f Class 3 races on last two outings; combination of return to 7f, ease in grade and much more favourable draw makes him of strong interest.
runners.comment: Ran respectably from wide stalls in two recent Class 3 sprints; big player in this grade
runners.owner: Eric Yeung Ting Fung & Angel Wong Sau Kuen

runners.name: Good Luck Babe
runners.spotlight: Out of form on dirt in recent months but now 7lb lower than when winning narrowly over C&D in October and is of renewed interest on this return to turf.
runners.comment: Out of form on dirt in recent months but this return to turf makes him of renewed interest

runners.name: Superb Kid
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner last season; hinted at possible return to form when fifth over 1m in February but has largely been disappointing for new stable this year.
runners.comment: Largely been disappointing for new stable this year

runners.name: Gorgeous Victory
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; placed over 6f last month and was denied clear run before keeping on for fifth over C&D a week ago; considered each-way.
runners.comment: Placed over 6f last month and was denied clear run when fifth over C&D a week ago

runners.name: Power Patch
runners.spotlight: Reserve; a down-the-field outsider on all four stats (6f-7f).
runners.comment: A down-the-field outsider on all four stats (6f-7f)

runners.name: Gallant Design
runners.spotlight: Placed on three of his five starts, the last twice over C&D off today's mark; drawn on the inside today and ought to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Placed on three of his five starts, the last twice over C&D off today's mark; shortlisted

runners.name: Ping Hai Comet
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has slipped to a dangerous mark but ought to have been suited by return to 1m3f when down the field at Happy Valley in March and may find this trip too sharp.
runners.comment: Has slipped to a dangerous mark but all his form is over 1m or further

runners.name: Lucky Man
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with emphatic C&D win on last month's stable debut and posted another good effort when keeping-on second behind Invictus Dragon here three weeks ago; must be considered in current mood.
runners.comment: Emphatic C&D winner on last month's stable debut and a good second her since

runners.name: Lovely Miles
runners.spotlight: Encountered traffic and wasn't disgraced when midfield on recent debut here (6f; wore cheekpieces) but is probably one for a bit further down the line; tried in blinkers over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on recent debut (6f) but probably one for further down the line
runners.owner: Keith Poon Pak Kit, Henry Cheng Wai Hong Et Al

runners.name: Ka Ying Superb
runners.spotlight: Suffered dip in form after finishing second over C&D on seasonal debut in September but took step back in right direction when sixth in this lower grade here (1m) three weeks ago and this drop back in trip will suit.
runners.comment: Took step back in right direction over 1m this month and this drop back to 7f will suit

runners.name: Invictus
runners.spotlight: Bettered mildly encouraging debut and shaped well when third behind Invictus Dragon and Lucky Man over C&D three weeks ago; has plenty of scope for further progress but outside draw dents his appeal.
runners.comment: Good third over C&D this month, his second start, but has outside draw today

runners.name: Prestige Emperor
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten over 6f on first two starts and has wide draw for this first attempt at 7f; swaps cheekpieces (worn once) for blinkers.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten over 6f on first two starts and has wide draw for this first attempt at 7f

runners.name: Joyful Joy
runners.spotlight: First foal from mare who was placed over 1m; came third in a 6f trial this month but is probably best watched on official debut; wears blinkers.
runners.comment: Placed in a 6f trial this month but probably best watched on official debut
runners.owner: Hong Kong Young Owner Racing Syndicate

runners.name: Invictus Dragon
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace to open account over C&D three weeks ago, his 11th start; did that fairly comfortably in the end and Zac Purton keeps the ride; can pose a potent threat off new mark.
runners.comment: Came from off the pace to open account C&D this month, doing it  comfortably in the end

runners.name: Circuit Fiery
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season and in the frame over C&D on all seven other starts this term, most recently when close second under Nichola Yuen last month; drawn wider and another 2lb higher today; still respected.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season and in the frame over C&D on all seven other starts this term
runners.owner: Jason Cheung Cho Yee & Cheung Ka Shing

races.race_name: The Sha Tin Vase (Group 3) (Course B) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Tomodachi Kokoroe
runners.spotlight: Career highlight when winning C&D Group 2 handicap in October; back on a workable mark now and will find this easier than last two assignments in level-weights Group races; could have a say.
runners.comment: Won Group 2 handicap over C&D in October and is back on a workable mark now; a possible

runners.name: Lucky With You
runners.spotlight: Yet to win this season and evidently on the downgrade now but ran well when fifth over C&D three weeks ago, having raced on the outside of the field, and is well drawn today; considered each-way.
runners.comment: On the downgrade now but ran quite well three weeks ago and has good draw; e-w claims

runners.name: Beauty Waves
runners.spotlight: Did well to win Happy Valley handicap from wide stall in February but is not the force of old and has become unreliable.
runners.comment: Won Happy Valley handicap in February but is not the force of old; others preferred

runners.name: Sky Trust
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season; runner-up off today's mark in March but not quite in same form lately; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season but not quite at best lately; others preferred
runners.owner: Bryan Kwan Hon Ming, Jessica Kwan Mun Hang Et Al

runners.name: Patch Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo who posted another career-best performance when beating Crimson Flash in Class 2 C&D handicap three weeks ago; has plum inside draw for first attempt at a Group race; likely contender.
runners.comment: Won Class 2 C&D handicap this month and has plum inside draw for first Group race; player

runners.name: Stellar Express
runners.spotlight: 6yo who has shown remarkable improvement over 5f here this year, with one of his three wins gained in a Group 3 handicap; not quite in same form lately, though, and this return to 6f is not ideal.
runners.comment: Rapid progress over 5f this year; not in same form lately, though, and 6f is not ideal

runners.name: Crimson Flash
runners.spotlight: Won two in row under prominent rides in March/April and, having started slowly, ended up with too much to do before finishing strongly to snatch second behind Patch Of Stars this month; not ideally drawn today but is probably still improving; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Won two in a row in March/April and did well to snatch second here this month; interesting

runners.name: Romantic Son
runners.spotlight: Gained this season's three wins in Class 3 handicaps at Happy Valley; ran poorly on dirt here this month and faces tough task from 10lb out of the weights today.
runners.comment: Gained this season's three wins in Class 3 handicaps; 10lb out of the weights for this

runners.name: Raging Blizzard
runners.spotlight: Consistent in Group 1/2 races over 6f/7f here since November, albeit without ever looking likely to cause Hong Kong's superstar sprinter Ka Ying Rising any trouble in those races; this won't be easy under top weight but he ought to give another good account.
runners.comment: This won't be easy under top weight but he's been consistent in Group 1/2 races this term

runners.name: Invincible Shield
runners.spotlight: Placed on both starts since narrow C&D win in January; would probably have preferred a stronger pace to aim at when keeping-on third here last time and may yet have a bigger run in him; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Placed on both starts since C&D win in January; may yet have a bigger run in him

races.race_name: The General Association Of Hong Kong Heyuan Societies Cup (Class 3) (Course B) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Family Jewel
runners.spotlight: Built upon satisfactory seasonal debut when fourth over C&D in March but three very underwhelming runs have followed; needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Fourth over C&D in March but three very underwhelming runs have followed
runners.owner: Tony Stradmoor, Billy Li Sze Kuen Et Al

runners.name: Complete Unknown
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all three Hong Kong starts but shaped as though well worth a crack at this longer trip when keeping-on fifth over 7f on last two appearances; sports first-time cheekpieces; worth a second look.
runners.comment: 0-3 in Hong Kong but recent efforts over 7f suggests he's well worth a crack at 7f

runners.name: Voyage Samurai
runners.spotlight: Badly out of form this season; drops in grade with Nichola Yuen claiming a very useful 10lb today but needs to stop the rot.
runners.comment: Badly out of form this season; drops in grade; needs to stop the rot

runners.name: Turin Champions
runners.spotlight: Made the frame off today's mark over C&D on last two outings (cheekpieces added last time); up in grade today but still has each-way claims.
runners.comment: In the frame over C&D on last two outings; up in grade; still considered each-way
runners.owner: Dr Sheldon Tse Hiu Tung, Tsoi Huk Yam & Ken Huang Jian Hua

runners.name: Everyone's Star
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed four times over 7f this season but was below form over that trip two weeks ago and is yet to convince stamina-wise over 1m.
runners.comment: Below form over 7f two weeks ago and yet to convince stamina-wise over 1m

runners.name: Winning Dragon
runners.spotlight: Made satisfactory seasonal debut at Happy Valley (1m1f) in October but wasn't seen again until heavy defeat on dirt this month; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Returned from layoff with heavy defeat on dirt last month; others have less to prove
runners.owner: Alexander Yeung Kwok Kai, Edward Yeung Et Al

runners.name: Another World
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped if judged on best of this season's dirt form but has finished well down the field on his last three turf starts (7f-1m).
runners.comment: Won on dirt in December but well down the field on last three turf starts

runners.name: Shanwah
runners.spotlight: Offered encouragement when sixth at big odds over C&D last month but was quickly back in the doldrums two weeks ago and has it all to prove again.
runners.comment: Penultimate run offered some hope but he was quickly back in the doldrums last time

runners.name: Supreme Agility
runners.spotlight: Largely in good form on turf/dirt in Class 4 this season but struggled when back in this grade this month and has the outside draw again.
runners.comment: Largely in good form in Class 4 this season but struggled in this grade this month
runners.owner: Rotary Club of Sha Tin Syndicate

runners.name: Trinity Treasure
runners.spotlight: Always behind on Hong Kong debut in March but ran big race from the front when fourth in first-time cheekpieces over C&D last month; has Zac Purton and first-time tongue-tie enlisted today; interesting.
