runners.comment: Well beaten on HK debut but ran big race from the front when fourth over C&D last month
runners.owner: Zhao Guoming, Xiao Bing, Annie An Ran & Tan Meng Sha

runners.name: Nucleozor
runners.spotlight: Made disappointing start to Hong Kong career for Danny Shum last season (four runs; 6f-7f) and made a low-key reappearance for new stable last month (7f); tries a new trip.
runners.comment: Made low-key stable debut last month and has now been unplaced all five Hong Kong starts

runners.name: Endued
runners.spotlight: Placed here on three of four starts since narrow C&D win in March; holds no secrets from the handicapper but will probably be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Placed on three of four starts since narrow C&D win in March; probably in the mix again

runners.name: Lucky Sam Gor
runners.spotlight: Began this year with two course wins (7f/1m); still in good form but has awkward draw today and needs to find hidden reserves to overcome current mark.
runners.comment: Dual course winner this year; needs to find hidden reserves to overcome current mark

runners.name: Goldfield
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who won a 7f maiden on the second of his two starts in Brazil; makes Hong Kong debut after nine-month absence.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo who won a 7f maiden on the second of his two starts in Brazil
runners.owner: Yue Yun Hing & Anita Yue Lam Chik Sun

runners.name: Amazing Partners
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who scored over C&D when upped in trip last month and probably bettered that performance when close second three weeks ago; 2lb higher and drawn much wider today but still considered.
runners.comment: Won over C&D when upped in trip last month and went close here three weeks ago; respected
runners.owner: Thomas Liang Cheung Biu & Elizabeth Lee Wong Sing Fung

runners.name: Mister Dapper
runners.spotlight: Reserve; late-maturing 6yo who posted career-best performance when close second to Amazing Partners over C&D last month; didn't fire last time, though.
runners.comment: Career-best form when close second here in April; didn't fire last time, though
runners.owner: P Chan Pak Hou, J Chan Chi Kwong & J Chan Chon San

races.race_name: Hong Kong Ka Ying (Meizhou) General Chamber Of Commerce Cup (Class 3) (Course B) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Dragon Four Seas
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well on seasonal debut in September but form nosedived afterwards, and there was no revival on recent stable debut.
runners.comment: Form nosedived after encouraging seasonal debut in September; has too much to prove
runners.owner: Dr Alan Lee Wai Kin, Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk Et Al

runners.name: Ka Ying Attack
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent 4yo who was placed over C&D on consective C&D starts in March/April and didn't run badly from wide stall here (7f) last time; more exposed than some but has good draw and current mark is workable.
runners.comment: Largely consistent 4yo; well drawn today and current mark is workable.

runners.name: New Future Folks
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over C&D towards end of last year but has been well below that form on recent outings and has double-digit draw today.
runners.comment: Well below best lately and has double-digit draw today

runners.name: Thousand Spirit
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in this higher grade since game C&D win in early April but disappointed on latest outing and, even if back on song, is probably vulnerable from stall 10.
runners.comment: Placed in this higher grade since game C&D win in April but disappointed on latest outing

runners.name: Young Emperor
runners.spotlight: Usually ridden patiently and will probably drop in from his wide stall; got going too late when fifth here last time but was placed on two previous outings and has each-way claims again if this race is run to suit.
runners.comment: In good form and has each-way claims if this race is run to suit usual patient tactics

runners.name: Majestic Valour
runners.spotlight: 2-3 over C&D in Class 4; found things tougher and managed only sixth when upped to this grade this month but may not have been ideally suited by the soft ground and remains lightly raced.
runners.comment: 2-3 over C&D in Class 4; held in this grade since but the ground was soft; still unexposed
runners.owner: Richard Yue Wah Chuk & David Pao

runners.name: Causeway King
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner when based in New Zealand but safely held (albeit from wide stalls) on both Hong Kong starts; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner when based in New Zealand but safely held on both Hong Kong starts

runners.name: Tactical Horizons
runners.spotlight: Well down the field at triple-digit odds on both Hong Kong starts (5f/6f).
runners.comment: Well down the field at triple-digit odds on both Hong Kong starts (5f/6f)
runners.owner: Elvis Kwok Kam Chuen, Winkle Lee Wing Ho Et Al

runners.name: Winning Buddies
runners.spotlight: Bettered inauspicious Hong Kong debut when midfield from wide stall over C&D this month and has much better draw today but others appeal a lot more all the same.
runners.comment: Second HK run was better than his debut but others make a lot more appeal all the same
runners.owner: 24/25 Jimmy K H Ting Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Crossborderpegasus
runners.spotlight: 3-5 last season (all wins over C&D), and has performed well from awkward stalls on all three appearances this term; not yet fully exposed and has better draw today; high on the list.
runners.comment: Has performed well from awkward draws this year and is not yet fully exposed; big player

runners.name: Cala Dei Mori
runners.spotlight: Smart 2yo when based in Italy; makes Hong Kong debut after seven-month absence but Zac Purton takes the ride.
runners.comment: Smart 2yo when based in Italy; Zac Purton rides on Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Do You Just
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in Hong Kong at fifth attempt, with clearcut Happy Valley win last month; further progress is possible but he's 7lb higher here and has a tough draw; hood removed from headgear combination.
runners.comment: Clearcut Happy Valley winner last month; 7lb higher here and drawn wide

runners.name: Jumbo Treasure
runners.spotlight: Reserve; front-runner who won three times over C&D in 2025; slightly disappointing on latest outing and current mark probably demands a career-best effort.
runners.comment: Slightly disappointing on latest outing and current mark probably demands a career best

runners.name: Smart Golf
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo who made virtually all over C&D in March, his third win, and did well to finish fourth from stall 13 here this month; drawn wide again today but still considered.
runners.comment: Improving 4yo; did well to finish fourth from stall 13 here this month; drawn wide again

runners.name: Tang Heart
runners.spotlight: 2-9 when trained in Australia, the latter win gained in 7f handicap in October; makes Hong Kong debut in a competitive race but booking of Joao Moreira catches the eye.
runners.comment: 2-9 when trained in Australia; makes Hong Kong debut with Joao Moreira booked

races.race_name: Prix La Fleche (Listed Race) (Turf)

runners.name: Aralia
runners.owner: Thierry Storme, Annua Racing SL et al

runners.name: Sacree Philomene
runners.owner: Ecurie Galopium Sas, Daytona Stable et al

races.race_name: Prix De Royaumont (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Behrayna
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to a couple of classy performers and yet to be beaten, winning a 1m2f110yd fillies newcomers race at Longchamp in April on good to soft before improving to land a 1m3f (very soft) Listed event at the same track, Zlata in second; extra furlong unlikely to be an issue and obvious claims for leading connections.
runners.comment: Yet to be beaten and won a Listed event last time, beating Zlata; obvious chance

runners.name: Zlata
runners.spotlight: Supplemented for this; won here on her debut over a mile on soft in September and improved from her seasonal return to finish just over 2l behind Behrayna in a 1m3f (very soft) Listed event almost a month ago; pedigree gives optimism for staying this trip and should play a part.
runners.comment: Just over 2l behind Behrayna last time and has scope for better

runners.name: Temapica
runners.spotlight: Won a fillies newcomers race at Saint-Cloud (1m2f, very soft) in March at long odds and then built on that with a third in a Listed event at Toulouse a month later, albeit a place behind Hatanka Fal; needs to step up again but more than capable of doing so, especially if a first try at this trip helps.
runners.comment: Lightly raced filly who steps up in trip; capable of improvement
runners.owner: AB Racing, Ecuries Sege Stempniak et al

runners.name: Marylene Du Serre
runners.spotlight: Took a while to get off the mark but won back-to-back races at Strasbourg at the tail-end of 2025, both over a mile on heavy ground; held on both starts this campaign and will need this 4f jump in trip to have a big effect to be involved; Cristian Demuro on for first time.
runners.comment: Needs a 4f jump in trip to have a significant effect; Cristian Demuro on for first time

runners.name: Hatanka Fal
runners.spotlight: Won two of races earlier in the year, including here on the AW, and run respectably in a couple of turf Listed events on her last two starts, finishing ahead of the less exposed Temapica last time; usually wears cheekpieces and half-sister stayed 1m4f, so might find extra for new trip.
runners.comment: Bit more exposed than some of her rivals but could improve for new trip

runners.name: Blanche De Medicis
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly who made a good start to her career when winning at Saint-Cloud over 7.5f on very soft last October; down the field but not beaten far on her 3yo return here on the AW but did better back at Saint-Cloud in a Group 3 after that; plenty of chance on pedigree (by Waldgeist) she'll enjoy this new trip, so hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Improved plenty from her 3yo return when last seen, and could step forward for new trip

races.race_name: Grand Prix De Chantilly (Group 2) (Turf)

runners.name: Eydon
runners.spotlight: Always had plenty of ability and recorded his two best figures since being tried over 1m4f, one of them resulting in an Italian Group 2 success; chased home subsequent Group 1 placed Santorini Star on his seasonal return at Newmarket, so sound claims on that effort; likes quick ground.
runners.comment: Already a Group 2 winner at this trip and chased home a good one recently; good chance

runners.name: Cualificar
runners.spotlight: Won four times in 2025 at up to 1m4f, the best of those being a win in the Group 2 Prix Niel, but posted a better figure than that when third on his seasonal return in the Prix d'Harcourt over 1m2f; going back up in trip will suit and makes plenty of appeal reunited with William Buick; seems to handle most ground.
runners.comment: Ran well on his seasonal return over 1m2f, and will be suited by going back up in trip

runners.name: Goliath
runners.spotlight: Biggest win to date came in the 2024 King George but did land another 1m4f Group 1 in September, beating the reliable Dubai Honour in Germany; can be a little in-and-out but produced a good effort in Doha in February behind a classy Japanese raider and sure to give a good account after a 106-day absence.
runners.comment: Dual Group 1 winner over this trip who hasn't been seen since a good run in Doha in Feb

runners.name: Espoir Avenir
runners.spotlight: Talented sort at his best, who has done the majority of his racing over further, including last time in a 1m7f Group 3 at Longchamp; probably capable of handling the drop in trip but faces some quality rivals, so hard to make a case for winning.
runners.comment: Should be able to handle the drop in trip, but doesn't appeal as the likely winner

runners.name: Oracle
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since his debut but his claim to fame so far is that he was the 1-2 market leader to beat Daryz in a conditions event a year ago, unsurprisingly finding that rival too good; beaten 10l last time in a Group 3, so needs a career best to land this, assuming the principals perform
runners.comment: Hasn't won since his debut and well beaten last time; hard task

runners.name: Zarraf
runners.spotlight: Capable of smart form but ran, by far, his best race when landing quite a strong 1m4f Listed event at Longchamp in April from the front; this is tougher but would clearly be dangerous if left alone in front.
runners.comment: Comes into this off clear career best, having made all; this is tougher unless left alone

races.race_name: Prix Du Gros-Chene - World Pool By PMU (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Sajir
runners.spotlight: Talented performer who has been tried up to 7f, but really sprung to prominence when landing a solid running of the Prix Maurice de Gheest (6.5f, good ground) last year; not remotely close to that level in three runs since, two of them when beaten at odds-on in Listed races, but this small field does give him a sound chance to bounce back; holds an entry in the Group 1 Queen Elizabeth II Jubilee Stakes.
runners.comment: Well below his Prix Maurice de Gheest winning level in three runs since; may bounce back

runners.name: Afjan
runners.spotlight: Another who had solid form as a 2yo, a lot of the time over this trip, including a Group 3 success at Longchamp; three starts this year have been steadily progressive and he sets the standard if matching his last run, when second in a 5f Group 3 at Longchamp, ahead of Lesslepasser and Ponntos.
runners.comment: Sets the standard if able to repeat the level of his last run, when ahead of two of these

runners.name: Moojeed
runners.spotlight: Had some very useful form as a juvenile, winning a 6f Group 3 here last June before not being beaten too far in the Group 2 Prix Robert Papin; wasn't seen after that for 268 days but made a promising return to action at this venue over half a furlong further than today; drop back to 5f probably not a problem and has more scope than some for better; holds and entry in the Group 1 Commonwealth Cup.
runners.comment: Ran well after a long absence last time and should cope with drop in trip; interesting

runners.name: Ponntos
runners.spotlight: Veteran front-runner who won this in 2024 and finished third 12 months ago, and far busier in 2026 than all his rivals, already winning a couple of times; not at his peak three weeks ago, but always capable of suddenly rediscovering his best and Christophe Soumillon rides for the first time.
runners.comment: Not at his peak three weeks ago but Christophe Soumillon rides for the first time

runners.name: Lesslepasser
runners.spotlight: Finished second in this last year, a place in front of Ponntos, when sent off 45-1, but not always consistent and looks more of a player for a place than the win; yet to land a Group race.
runners.comment: Yet to win a Group race but was second in this 12 months ago

races.race_name: Qatar Prix du Jockey Club (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)

runners.name: Constitution River
runners.spotlight: Short-headed in a hot Newmarket maiden last July before making all when odds-on in both a Galway maiden (7f, yielding) last July and a Group 3 at the Curragh the following month; pedigree suggested longer trips would suit and he impressed when pulling 7l clear in the Dee Stakes at Chester (10.5f, good; third has since won a Listed race) 24 days ago; connections opted to come here instead of Epsom and he holds leading claims, although stall 15 makes things difficult for Ryan Moore.
runners.comment: 3-4; impressed when winning the Dee Stakes by 7l on his return; stall 15 makes it tough
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Hankelow
runners.spotlight: Progressive colt who won the Autumn Stakes at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) last October; posted an improved effort when third of 13 in the French 2,000 Guineas (very soft) three weeks ago and his pedigree suggests he should stay this longer trip; could go well from stall 1 but his trainer has made it known softer is ground is preferable.
runners.comment: Posted an improved effort when third in the French Guineas three weeks ago; up in trip
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Hawk Mountain
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-4 when winning the Group 1 Futurity Trophy Stakes at Doncaster (1m, heavy; made virtually all) in October and made a successful return here over 1m1f under Christophe Soumillon this month (first-time cheekpieces which are now replaced by blinkers); should have no problem with this longer trip but stall 11 is a concern.
runners.comment: Made it 4-5 when winning here on his return; trip should be fine but draw is a concern
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Komorebi
runners.spotlight: Won twice last year and posted an improved effort when winning a Group 3 at Longhchamp (1m, good) last month; took another step forward when second in the French 2,000 Guineas three weeks ago, shaping as if this longer trip would be within range; good claims for Andre Fabre who is bidding to win this for the first time since 2015.
runners.comment: Shaped as if this trip would be in range when second in the French Guineas last time

runners.name: Oxagon
runners.spotlight: Unsuited by heavy ground when last of five in the Futurity Trophy Stakes at Doncaster last year, but he took well to first-time cheekpieces when landing the Craven (1m, good; made virtually all) on his reappearance; that form hasn't worked out and he could only plug on for sixth in the 2,000 Guineas next time; supplemented for 72,000euros and represents the same connections as 2020 winner Mishriff, but has stamina to prove for this far.
runners.comment: Won the Craven on his return but only sixth in the 2,000 Guineas; supplemented for this

runners.name: A Boy Named Susie
runners.spotlight: Won on his debut at Killarney (1m, soft) last summer and has posted some good efforts in Group company since, including when runner-up in a Group 1 at Saint-Cloud (1m2f, very soft) in October; ran well in the Ballysax Stakes at Leopardstown (1m2f, good to yielding) on his reappearance 49 days ago and has a chance of hitting the frame over this slightly longer trip.
runners.comment: Has posted some good efforts since winning on his debut, including on his reappearance

runners.name: Montreal
runners.spotlight: Stepped forward from his debut to win a maiden by 8l at Leopardstown (1m, good) last August, and posted a good effort here in a first-time tongue-tie on his reappearance, narrowly beaten by Dolmalan having had the run of things; open to further improvement after just three starts and he's a contender.
runners.comment: Narrowly beaten by Dolmalan here on his reappearance last month; open to more improvement

runners.name: Campacite
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year, including a Listed event at Deauville (1m, good to soft), and he was runner-up in the Criterium International (1m, very soft) in October; behind Komorebi at Longchamp (1m, good) on his return and third to Hawk Mountain here over 1m1f 27 days ago; should stay this far but better is needed.
runners.comment: Behind Hawk Mountain when third here over 1m1f 27 days ago; should stay this far
runners.owner: Guy Pariente Holding, G Augustin-Normand & Ecurie Friedrider

runners.name: Gostam
runners.spotlight: Germain raider who was 3-3 as a 2yo and maintained his unbeaten record when landing a Group 2 at Munich (1m2f, good) on this month's reappearance; open to further improvement but it will be needed.
runners.comment: Germain raider who maintained his unbeaten record when landing a Group 2 on his return

runners.name: Dolmalan
runners.spotlight: Tongue-tied for all three starts; made a winning debut at Fontainebleau (1m1f, soft) in November and took his record to 3-3 when quickening up from the back of the field off a steady pace to beat Montreal in a Listed race here over 1m2f (good) last month; this unbeaten colt is open to further progress and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Made it 3-3 when winning a Listed event here over 1m2f last month; on the shortlist
runners.owner: H H The Aga Khan's Stud Sc

runners.name: Alam
runners.spotlight: Tongue-tied for all four starts; beaten a head on his turf debut at Saint-Cloud (10.5f, soft) last month and went one better in a Group 3 over that same C&D (soft) 26 days ago; however, his yard probably has stronger claims with Dolmalan and he could find things tough from the widest stall.
runners.comment: Got off the mark in a Group 3 at Saint-Cloud last time; widest stall makes things tough

runners.name: Canadian
runners.spotlight: Beaten 2l when sixth of nine at Longchamp (1m2f, good to soft) on last month's debut but he finished well held when filling that same position at Saint-Cloud (1m2f, heavy) 16 days ago; poor claims in this much deeper contest.
runners.comment: Beaten 2l on his debut but well held on heavy ground last time; poor claims

runners.name: Daryzan
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to last year's Arc winner Daryz for these same connections; created a good impression when landing a newcomers event on his debut at Saint-Cloud (1m, soft; tongue-tied) 26 days ago, coming 5l clear in the style of a nice prospect; draw could have been kinder and likely to face quicker ground today, but should be well suited by this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Half-brother to Daryz; looked a nice prospect when winning over 1m at Saint-Cloud on debut
runners.owner: H H The Aga Khan's Stud Sc

runners.name: Segall
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form when finishing runner-up here on the AW over 1m1f in March before going one better in a Group 3 at Longchamp (1m1f, good to soft) next time; well beaten in the French 2,000 Guineas three weeks ago and better will be needed now stepping up in trip; Andre Fabre has stronger claims with Komorebi.
runners.comment: Won a Group 3 at Longchamp (1m1f) last month but well beaten in the French 2,000 Guineas

runners.name: Pearled Majesty
runners.spotlight: Progressive sort who landed a comfortable success at Saint-Cloud (1m2f, very soft) on his reappearance in March, and followed up in a Group 3 at Longchamp (10.5f, good) 49 days ago; open to further improvement and holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Won comfortably on his return and followed up in a Group 3 last time; each-way claims
runners.owner: Mme Christiane Head & Mauricio Delcher Sanchez

runners.name: Gusto
runners.spotlight: Hooded and tongue-tied for all four starts; beat Alam here on the AW in January (9.5f) before another solid effort in a Listed race next time; close-up third on his turf debut in a Group 3 at Longchamp (1m1f, good to soft) 56 days ago but better will be needed from a tough draw on his first go in a Group 1.
runners.comment: 1-4; close-up third in a Group 3 on his turf debut but better needed from a tough draw

races.race_name: Prix De Sandringham (Group 2) (Fillies) (Turf)

runners.name: Mubasimah
runners.spotlight: 7f debut winner last September, who improved plenty on that to be runner-up in the Group 3 Oh So Sharp Stakes; ran to a similar level on her return when down the field in the Nell Gwyn before only beating four home in the 1,000 Guineas, her opening try over a mile; this will be her first start away from the Rowley Mile at Newmarket and only raced on quick ground, but remains with potential.
runners.comment: Only ever raced on Newmarket's July course and quick ground; second start over a mile

runners.name: Vaticana
runners.spotlight: Been in pretty good heart this year, notably landing a mile Listed event on heavy ground at Saint-Cloud in March, but wasn't anywhere near that level in the Prix Vanteaux when last seen 56 days ago; capable of good form but left with something to prove.
runners.comment: Good chance if back to the level of her Listed race success, but something to prove again

runners.name: Pierchic
runners.spotlight: Showed some useful form last year for Charlie Appleby on the AW over 7f, and recorded her best effort for this trainer when third in the Group 3 Prix de la Grotte (1m, good) under this jockey behind a subsequent 1,000 Guineas runner-up; easy enough to forgive last run considering she didn't get a clear path over this C&D, so leading claims on previous start.
runners.comment: Didn't get a clear run over C&D last time but big chance on effort prior to that

runners.name: Green Sense
runners.spotlight: Had quite a busy time of it as a 2yo over mostly sprint trips, notably landing the Group 2 Prix Robert Papin here in July; went on to be Group 1 placed before a modest effort in the Goffs Million during September, a first venture past 6f, and not seen since; pedigree doesn't offer tons of hope for staying a mile but it's interesting connections are choosing this as a starting point.
runners.comment: Had some good form as a 2yo, but not seen since September and has stamina to prove

runners.name: Royal Chapel
runners.spotlight: Won two races as a 2yo, including a mile Listed event at Bordeaux Le Bouscat on soft ground in a first-time hood, but improved on that on the figures when third on her seasonal return in another Listed contest at Longchamp back down over 7f; going back up in trip should be a positive and can be involved.
runners.comment: Won two of her four starts and produced a pb on seasonal return; can be involved

runners.name: Miuccia
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f conditions event at Deauville (good to soft) last year and ran with credit in a couple of Group races after, including at this track; improved plenty from her seasonal return when running here over 1m1f fairly recently, but little doubt she'll need more to land a race at this level.
runners.comment: Bit more exposed than some but did run well here over 1m1f just over a month ago

runners.name: Demah
runners.spotlight: Only made her debut in April, when she won a mile newcomers race over C&D by 2l, well in command inside the final half a furlong; half-sister to smart stablemate Sahlan and could easily play a part here despite a big jump up in class.
runners.comment: Hard to judge accurately but won a C&D newcomers race easily in April; big jump in grade

runners.name: Naomis
runners.spotlight: Showed potential earlier in her career and then secured her second win when landing a mile Listed event at Toulouse last month on good to soft, beating a rival who then ran respectably in the Poule d'Essai Des Poulains; this requires more but it isn't a strong race for the level.
runners.comment: Beat a smart colt last time but this still demands more, albeit this is a weak event

runners.name: Lemon Muffin
runners.owner: Aaron Sones, Julie Gilbert & Harrison M Sones

runners.name: Om N Joy
runners.owner: Baker Stables LLC, Michael Golovko & Terrence J Scanlan

races.race_name: Horn & Company German 1000 Guineas (Group 2) (3yo Fillies) (Turf)
